2026-06-30.headline: Last day of Q2 + end of month — institutions rebalancing, semis still anchored. AVAV +30% on drone earnings blowout. TSM $62.4M call block is one of the biggest ever surfaced. CIEN climbing the DP board. AWS Summit keynote 11am. JOLTS + Consumer Confidence at 10am. NKE earnings tonight.
2026-06-30.overview: Today is the last trading day of Q2 and the last day of the month at the same time — that double-event means institutions are cleaning up books, locking in gains, and window dressing into mega caps before the quarter closes. Volume tends to be amplified on these days, especially in the afternoon. Nasdaq futures are leading (+0.24%) driven by continued semiconductor momentum. AeroVironment (AVAV) is the morning's big winner — up 30%+ on a massive earnings beat (EPS $1.84 vs $1.41, revenue $641.6M vs $556.4M) with strong drone systems demand. Oil has now fallen to $71 as the Iran ceasefire holds and Strait of Hormuz traffic is increasing for the first time since the attacks — a huge relief for inflation and the broader market. Nike and Constellation Brands report after close tonight. NFP comes Thursday July 2 (a day early because of Friday's July 4 holiday). Chicago PMI at 9:45am and JOLTS + Consumer Confidence both hit at 10am.
2026-06-30.market_context: Quarter-end is the theme. When Q2 closes today, institutions will have finished one of the best quarters in years for AI and semiconductor stocks. MU led dark pool flow Monday at $6.7B ask-side — memory demand is still the engine. NVDA clustered another $1.0B single print. The big story from the options desk: a $62.4M call block hit on TSM $400C September 18 — one of the largest single call positions the platform has surfaced since launch. Someone is making a massive directional bet that Taiwan Semi runs to $400 by September. CIEN (Ciena) is also quietly climbing into the top 3 of dark pool prints for the first time ($524M ask Monday) — watch for a build-up there. The other key catalyst today is Amazon's AWS Summit in Washington D.C. with the keynote around 11am ET — any AI/cloud infrastructure announcements could move AMZN and the whole cloud sector. Iran ceasefire continuing to hold and oil at $71 from $95+ weeks ago is the macro tailwind that has quietly done a lot of work for the market.

setups.flow: Earnings beat, bullish kicker, +30% pre-mkt, drones
setups.why: Failed 2U but with a bullish kicker — the earnings beat completely overrides the bearish strat signal. AeroVironment crushed Q4: EPS $1.84 vs $1.41, revenue $641.6M vs $556.4M, strong drone bookings with 1.4x book-to-bill. End-of-quarter institutions love a confirmed earnings winner. Multiple ORB entries will develop — wait for the 10-min or 30-min confirmation, don't chase the open.

Outside bar range expansion with a conference today. Oracle has been quietly building as a major AI cloud data center player. Quarter-end window dressing loves mega cap AI names. NVDA $1.0B bid DP Monday says the AI cluster is still getting bought — ORCL sits in that same narrative. Outside bar means it's already moving.

setups.flow: $6.7B ask-side total flow Monday, $396M single print
setups.why: Failed 2D bullish reversal with the largest total dark pool flow of any stock Monday at $6.7B ask-side. Memory demand is the bedrock of the AI trade — NVDA, AVGO, and hyperscalers all need MU's chips. Quarter-end institutions are clustering (confirmed fewer than 3 prints visible — accumulation mode). $6.7B doesn't flow ask-side on the last day of Q2 by accident.

setups.flow: $1.0B ask-side AH Monday (biggest single name), clustering
setups.why: Outside bar while clustering — NVDA is in range expansion mode with institutions still accumulating on every dip. $1.0B single print Monday after hours. GTC Taipei wrapped last week and the institutional buy-the-dip behavior never stopped. Quarter-end = window dressing into the best-performing mega cap of Q2.

setups.flow: Current mover, +11.34%, Joined Open Compute Project
setups.why: Outside bar on a current mover. Enphase jumped 11% after joining the Open Compute Project as a Platinum Member — connecting solar/energy hardware to the AI data center power story. Range expansion in progress.

setups.flow: Dividend in 10 days, Jensen $1T call still in play
setups.why: Outside bar following last week's massive move on Jensen Huang's trillion-dollar company call. Dividend in 10 days adds institutional buy pressure. Range expansion continues.
Nokia Failed 2D bullish reversal with dark pool on the board. Telecom infrastructure play that has been building quietly. Clean strat setup into quarter-end.

Bloom Energy inside bar compressed with confluence showing across timeframes. Energy storage/fuel cell play that benefits from AI data center power demand. Inside bars that show multi-timeframe confluence often produce clean breaks.
Raytheon inside bar with dark pool on the board. Defense name that benefits from continued Iran/Middle East geopolitical backdrop. Compressed and waiting for direction.

Failed 2U is a bearish reversal signal but Microsoft has a conference today and $324M bid-side dark pool Monday. Microsoft Build last week set up new AI platform announcements — the conference today could provide follow-through. Watch if the institution support holds or the bearish strat signal plays out.

setups.flow: Showing confluence, buying Iridium to challenge SpaceX/Amazon
setups.why: Rocket Lab outside bar with confluence showing — and a major catalyst just dropped: RKLB announced it is buying Iridium to directly compete with SpaceX and Amazon in satellite services. That changes the scale of Rocket Lab's addressable market significantly. Outside bar + fresh catalyst + confluence is a clean setup.

bulls.why: AeroVironment blew out fiscal Q4 — EPS $1.84 vs $1.41, revenue $641.6M vs $556.4M, 1.4x book-to-bill. Drone systems demand from defense spending is the catalyst. Fiscal 2027 EBITDA guide came in below est but investors focused on drone demand strength.
Enphase Energy joined the Open Compute Project as a Platinum Member, connecting their solar/energy hardware to the AI data center power infrastructure ecosystem.
Rocket Lab announced it is buying Iridium to compete with SpaceX and Amazon in satellite services. Transformational deal that expands RKLB from launch services into satellite network operations.
Abivax reported positive Phase 3 maintenance Part 2 results for Obefazimod (inflammatory bowel disease drug). Biotech catalyst on clinical data.
Comcast plans to spin off NBCUniversal — a major strategic pivot. Reuters also reported Comcast is weighing a video-game market entry after the cable/connectivity business spins off.

bears.why: Concentrix cut full-year earnings AND revenue guidance, citing slower AI-driven customer experience spending. Investors had high hopes for AI monetization — the guide-down crushed them.
Vishay Intertechnology priced a $750 million common stock offering — heavy dilution for shareholders. Stock offering of this size on a mid-cap always hits the price.
Bitcoin fell 2.2% to ~$59,200 and analysts are cutting price targets. MicroStrategy is a pure leveraged Bitcoin proxy — when BTC drops, MSTR drops harder.
Fortinet downgraded to Reduce from Hold at HSBC. Cybersecurity sector still under pressure from the Zscaler margin warning earlier this month.
Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley both cut to Underperform at Oppenheimer. Financial sector seeing some quarter-end rotation pressure.

trump_stocks.news: Trump bought up to $5M in Axon (formerly Taser) stock personally before a $220M ICE contract bid was awarded. Government contract + insider-adjacent presidential buying.

morning_prep.theme: End of Q2 + End of Month. The biggest lesson from this week isn't about making money — it's about discipline. TSLA hit 2000%+ option gains yesterday. AVAV is up 30% this morning. If you missed those, that's fine. There is always another play. The goal is consistency, not catching every home run. Quarter-end historically produces strong afternoon trends and powerful closing moves — but it is NOT guaranteed. Let price prove itself before entering anything.
rules: No overleveraging
No FOMO — no chasing yesterday's winners
No forcing trades before 10am data
Wait for ORB confirmation (10-min or 30-min)
Use ADR highs/lows for targets and scaling out
Respect your stop, respect your portfolio
Consolidation ≠ trade over — good trends give multiple entries
morning_prep.market: 10:00 AM ET is the key time today — JOLTS + Consumer Confidence both drop at the same time. Expect volatility at the open and around the data. DO NOT force trades before the reaction. Afternoon is where quarter-end and month-end moves typically show up — institutions rebalancing, window dressing, powerful closing drives. Watch SPY for institutional buying or selling after 10am. AWS Summit keynote around 11am ET is a secondary catalyst for AMZN and cloud names.

setups.catalyst: ★ Earnings blowout — bullish kicker. Higher timeframe resistance remains.
setups.bull: 10-min or 30-min ORB above 190 with volume. Do NOT blindly buy because of earnings — wait for confirmation + volume clearing resistance.
setups.note: ★ Great trends give multiple entries. Miss the open? You still get 15-min ORB, 30-min ORB, inside bar, 2↑2 continuation, consolidation breakout. Never assume you missed it.

setups.catalyst: Analyst initiated Overweight. History of strong unusual options flow.
setups.bull: Weekly resistance break + 2↑2 continuation + ORB confirmation with volume. Only if market confirms.
setups.note: Positive technical structure. Do not enter without market confirmation first.

setups.catalyst: AWS Summit keynote ~11am ET — new AI and cloud infrastructure announcements.
setups.bull: AI headlines, cloud news, momentum off keynote. Unusual options volume flagged — use new filter + heatmap to find the active strikes.
setups.note: Unusual options activity already showing. AMZN $310M bid DP Monday. Keynote is a known time-stamped catalyst.

setups.catalyst: Russell 2000 reconstitution today. Very compressed structure.
setups.bull: Large move possible if price escapes the compression. Quarter-end rebalancing could be the trigger.
setups.note: Remain patient. Do not anticipate direction — let price break and confirm first.

setups.catalyst: Long consolidation, monthly reversal, weekly Doji, unusual options flow in calls.
setups.bull: Breakout toward overhead gaps above 15.34. Weekly resistance break + ORB confirmation.
setups.note: Longer-term setup. Only if market confirms direction.

setups.catalyst: Earnings after close. Expected move ±8%.
setups.bull: Historically gaps significantly after earnings. Treat as earnings gamble only.
setups.bear: Expected range down to $38 on a miss.
setups.note: Earnings gamble — risk only what you are comfortable losing entirely. Not a directional trade.

events.detail: June Chicago PMI — last print was 62.7 rebound from April's 49.2 contraction. Trajectory matters heading into NFP.
JOLTS Job Openings (April) + CB Consumer Confidence — last JOLTS came in hot at 7.6M. First labor data point before NFP Thursday.
April FHFA Housing Price Index at 8:30am · April S&P Case-Shiller Home Price Index at 9:00am
Amazon AWS Summit in Washington D.C. — keynote around 11am ET. New cloud and AI infrastructure announcements.
MSFT, ORCL, NIO, F, GM, STLA all at conferences today
NET, MDB, DVN, TCOM, TME annual meetings today
NKE (Nike) — expected move ±8% · STZ (Constellation Brands)
Last day of Q2 and end of month — Russell 2000 reconstitution today. NFP drops Thursday 7/2 (day early due to July 4 holiday).

2026-06-03.headline: AVGO earnings AMC — the AI infrastructure verdict. White House drops new 10-12.5% tariff proposal, oil at $95.79. MRVL +13% premarket continuing yesterday's best day since 2000. GME +12% on earnings blowout + $2B buyback. Heavy data: ADP 8:15am, ISM Services 10am, Beige Book 2pm.
2026-06-03.overview: Biggest earnings night of the week is tonight — Broadcom (AVGO) reports after close and it's the single most important AI infrastructure print of this earnings season. MRVL is still flying +13% pre-market after yesterday's best day since 2000 (+32%), riding Jensen Huang's 'next trillion-dollar company' call from GTC Taipei. GameStop blew out earnings: EPS $0.30 vs $0.16, revenue $835M vs $681M, 65% collectibles growth, and a $2B buyback — up 12%. The macro picture got more complicated overnight: the White House dropped a new tariff proposal (10-12.5% on major trading partners) after the Supreme Court struck down the old tariff authority, and Iran tensions triggered another US military exchange pushing oil to $95.79. Nasdaq futures are the only green index (+0.23%) this morning thanks to AI strength holding. Three macro prints today: ADP jobs at 8:15am, ISM Services + Factory Orders at 10am, Fed Beige Book at 2pm.
2026-06-03.market_context: Two forces pulling in opposite directions. AI hardware is still ripping — MRVL, NVDA, and AVGO are all strong, and tonight AVGO's AI revenue number plus any commentary on the $36B Anthropic/Google TPU deal will tell you how big hyperscaler AI spend is for the next 18 months. That is the number. On the other side, the macro is getting messy: the new tariff proposal is live, oil is at $95.79 after Iran strikes, and private equity names (KKR -5.4%, BX, APO) got hit after Bloomberg reported a Swiss PE fund capping withdrawals — a contagion fear for the PE sector. The tariff news is directly bullish for steel (NUE, X) and domestic manufacturers but bearish for autos and anything with global supply chains. INTC had a good day yesterday — called TSM a 'trusted partner not rival,' easing competition fears from the NVDA RTX Spark announcement. The IEF Treasury ETF had the biggest DP print of Tuesday at $863M ask-side — institutions selling bonds, which means rates are pushing higher again. Watch the Beige Book at 2pm for any fresh stagflation language.

setups.why: Failed 2U going into earnings after close. $3.2B dark pool on Tuesday with $771M MU bid and $291M MRVL bid alongside it — the whole AI chip cluster is being watched closely. AVGO's AI networking chip revenue and commentary on the $36B Anthropic/Google TPU deal is the single most important thing happening tonight. Direction comes from the print.

Failed 2D bullish reversal. Intel ripped 5.36% yesterday after calling TSM a 'trusted partner not rival' — that directly addressed the fear that NVDA's ARM-based RTX Spark chip would obsolete INTC's partnerships. Confluence showing across timeframes. Conference catalyst already played and stock held the move.

Failed 2D bullish reversal. Tesla has been compressing while the rest of AI names ran. $311M ask-side dark pool Tuesday. The new tariff proposal for 50% American Content Rule for Autos is actually bullish for Tesla — as a domestic EV manufacturer, it doesn't need to import parts the way STLA and GM do.

Outside bar range expansion with ex-dividend tomorrow as the near-term catalyst. Qualcomm has been under pressure from NVDA's RTX Spark chip but the outside bar says the range is expanding. Dividend could attract buyers today ahead of the ex-date.

Failed 2U is bearish but CoreWeave just raised $900M in a junk bond sale — that is aggressive capital raising to fund AI data center expansion. Conference today. The capital raise shows confidence but the junk bond label means high risk premium. Watch the conference for direction.

Nebius has a conference today. Failed 2U is a bearish reversal signal, but NBIS has been a current mover with Aschenbrenner's AI fund holding 5.6%. Conference today is the catalyst — if they show strong AI infrastructure demand it could override the pattern.

Inside bar compressing into the annual shareholder meeting tomorrow. INTC's comment about TSM being a 'trusted partner' was a positive signal. Taiwan Semi is the backbone of AI chip production — Jensen's $100B/year Taiwan spending announcement from GTC Taipei is a direct tailwind. Meeting tomorrow could bring guidance updates.

SanDisk compressing inside bar with a conference next Tuesday as the catalyst. Memory and storage name riding the AI data boom. Inside bar says it is coiled — direction comes from Tuesday's conference.

setups.flow: AH flow last night, earnings BMO tomorrow
setups.why: Ciena had notable after-hours flow last night and reports earnings tomorrow before the open. Optical networking name — same theme as LITE and MRVL. The AI data center buildout is driving massive demand for optical interconnects. Worth watching ahead of the BMO print.

bulls.why: GameStop blowout — EPS $0.30 vs $0.16, revenue $835M vs $681M (+14%), collectibles sales +65%, free cash flow $333M, $2B buyback announced. Strongest quarter in years.
Marvell had its best day since 2000 on Tuesday (+32%) after Jensen called it 'the next trillion-dollar company.' Still running +13% pre-market today. AI networking chips in full momentum.
Intel called Taiwan Semiconductor a 'trusted partner, not rival' — directly easing competition fears from NVDA's ARM-based RTX Spark chip. Showed Intel's fab and design relationships remain intact.
Medtronic beat with revenue $9.81B, raised full-year guidance. Medical device strength continuing.
Macy's strongest Q1 growth in four years — revenue $4.68B vs $4.61B est, comp sales +3%, Bloomingdale's 7th straight growth quarter, raised full-year guidance.
Broadcom is a current mover ahead of tonight's earnings. $3.2B dark pool Tuesday, $36B Anthropic/Google TPU deal backstop, AI networking chip dominance. This is the AI infrastructure verdict.

bears.why: GitLab beat EPS ($0.23 vs $0.21) and raised guidance but announced a 14% workforce reduction (350 people), exiting 22 countries, and $30-35M restructuring charges. Market punishing the restructuring.
Private equity sector hit after Bloomberg reported Partners Group (Swiss PE) capped withdrawals from a fund. Contagion fear across PE names — BX and APO also down.
HP Enterprise digesting its massive earnings blowout gap. Stock soared 26% yesterday — some profit-taking normal after that kind of move.
White House pushing a 50% American Content Rule for Autos — directly hits Stellantis which imports a large portion of parts. Tariff/trade policy headwind.
Palo Alto beat (EPS 85c vs 80c, revenue $3B vs $2.94B, NGS ARR +60% to $8.1B) but stock fell as it digests the massive rally from March lows. Good earnings, exhausted chart.

trump_stocks.news: White House pushing 50% American Content Rule for Autos — direct Trump tariff/domestic manufacturing policy hitting foreign auto importers
White House dropped new 10-12.5% tariff proposal on major trading partners after Supreme Court struck down old authority — steel names are direct tariff beneficiaries
Same new White House tariff proposal — US Steel and domestic producers benefit from higher import barriers
Oil at $95.79 (+2.2%) after another US-Iran military exchange overnight — Trump's Middle East foreign policy driving energy prices higher
IBM commits $10B+ to quantum computing — aligns directly with Trump's executive push for US leadership in quantum technology and domestic AI/tech infrastructure

trump_context: Big tariff day. The White House dropped a new 10-12.5% tariff proposal on major trading partners after the Supreme Court struck down their old tariff authority — they came back with a new legal approach. Steel (NUE, X) benefits directly. Autos are getting hit — STLA is down 3% on a separate '50% American Content Rule for Autos' push. Oil hit $95.79 after another US-Iran military exchange, which is a Trump Middle East policy consequence feeding directly into energy prices.

IBM committed $10B+ to quantum computing overnight — that aligns with Trump's executive orders on US quantum leadership and domestic tech sovereignty. Energy names (XOM, CVX, OXY, LNG) are getting a tailwind from $95 oil. Watch steel names into the week as the new tariff proposal headline settles.

events.detail: ADP Employment Report — second jobs print of the week into Friday Payrolls
ISM Services Index + Factory Orders — stagflation read with oil at $95
Fed Beige Book — watch for stagflation language ahead of June 17 FOMC
AVGO (Broadcom) — AI infrastructure verdict, $36B Anthropic/TPU deal · CRWD (CrowdStrike)

2026-05-22.headline: China broker crackdown triggers mass ADR selloff — FUTU -39%, TIGR -37%; BABA, BIDU, PDD, NIO gapping red in unison. US/Iran deal hopes + mega-cap bid keep broader tape constructive into Memorial Day.
2026-05-22.overview: Stock futures are slightly higher going into Memorial Day weekend. The US and Iran are talking about a possible deal, which is helping. BUT — China's government cracked down on brokers that let mainland Chinese investors trade US stocks. That sent FUTU crashing -39% and TIGR -37%, and it's dragging down all Chinese stocks listed in the US (BABA, PDD, BIDU, NIO, XPEV all dropping). At 10am ET, two things hit at once: consumer confidence data (already near all-time lows) AND a Fed official speaking — so expect a bumpy open around that time.
2026-05-22.market_context: Big tech and AI stocks are still running the show. The top 5 mega-cap stocks are outperforming the rest of the market by the biggest gap since 2003 — which means a few big names are carrying the whole index. Software stocks are bouncing back after Workday and Zoom both beat earnings, pushing back against fears that software spending was dying. Oil is still high at ~$100/barrel, which matters because it can push inflation back up and force the Fed to raise rates again. Watch for news on OpenAI potentially filing for an IPO today — that could spark a big AI/tech rally.

2026-05-26.headline: Iran peace deal hopes send futures flying — Nasdaq +1.1%, semis and AI leading. Overnight US strikes + Strait of Hormuz fight keep the ceasefire fragile. Market reopens at all-time highs.
2026-05-26.overview: Futures are up this morning because the US and Iran look close to a deal that would reopen oil shipping lanes through the Strait of Hormuz. That is pushing oil prices down 4% and sending chip stocks higher — Micron is up over 6% pre-market after UBS tripled their price target to $1,675. The Nasdaq is up 1.1%. But be careful: the US launched new self-defense strikes overnight and the deal is not signed yet. The S&P just closed its 8th straight up week with the Dow back above 50K, so we are reopening at record highs. AutoZone is dropping 5% pre-market after weak international sales. Consumer Confidence at 10am is the number to watch today.
2026-05-26.market_context: Two things in focus: (1) The Iran deal — if it holds, oil stays low, inflation fears ease, and the whole market benefits especially AI and semis. (2) Record-high tape — the market has been going straight up 8 weeks in a row, so any bad headline (deal falls apart, ugly Consumer Confidence) could cause a sharper drop than normal. Bigger week ahead: PCE inflation and Q1 GDP on Friday, plus Costco, Salesforce, and Snowflake earnings. OKLO also in the news — the DOE selected them for a nuclear fuel program. Watch oil, Iran headlines, and the 10am Consumer Confidence print closely.

setups.why: Beat earnings but sold off -4.42% Fri on sell-the-news. Dark pool loaded back in hard. TD Cowen fireside Wed — Zelnick could drop fresh GTA VI color. Sweeps already positioned for it.
AI data center landlord signed $7.5B hyperscaler lease, now past 1 GW contracted. Dip -4.48% Fri got bought hard by dark pool. Up +3.7% pre-market. PURPLE confluence, Signals Aligned.

Crashed -27% Fri on China regulator fine + shutdown order. Gapping up this morning but options are screaming bearish — 93% puts, 14x more put $ than calls. Earnings Thu. Dead-cat risk. Let price action decide.

Ripped +5.27% Fri on $375M DoC quantum award + AI/photonics roadmap. Susquehanna upgraded to $100. Strat candle says bearish reversal but flow, options, and catalyst all lean bullish. Divergence — watch the open.
Cruise heading into peak Memorial Day travel season. Prints leaned bid-side Fri but sweeps lean calls. Record booking demand. Gapping +2.1% pre-market. RED divergence — wait for a clear direction.

setups.flow: $420M flow, 2.1x surge, split $169M ask vs $207M bid
setups.why: Won 5-yr SAP migration deal. Big flow but genuine coin flip — dark pool split almost evenly. Signals Mixed. Size is there, let price action decide direction.
Shareholder meeting today. Investor event 6/1. Close to a 3-2 trigger on the monthly — pharma beaten down on patent-cliff fears while structure is setting up. Multi-timeframe play.
Direct-to-phone satellite racing to deploy 45 satellites this year, AT&T/Verizon as partners. SpaceX IPO keeps space sector in spotlight. 3-bar on the monthly — longer timeframe setup.

bulls.why: UBS triples price target to $1,675. Semis rally on Iran deal + AI memory demand. Trump praised Micron US investment plans.

2026-05-27.headline: MU hits $1 trillion — AI infrastructure steamroller keeps rolling. Zscaler -23% tanks cybersecurity sector. Four AMC reports tonight: MRVL, CRM, HPQ, SNOW.
2026-05-27.overview: Futures are still going up on two things: the Iran deal getting closer (oil dropped another 3.8% to $90) and AI spending showing no signs of slowing — Micron hit a $1 trillion market cap yesterday. Nasdaq is up 0.66%, and the Russell 2000 is up 0.78% meaning smaller stocks are now joining the party too. BUT: Zscaler tanked 23% after hours because it cut its cash flow margin guidance — that is dragging down every cybersecurity stock premarket (Palo Alto -5%, CrowdStrike -4%, SentinelOne -5.5%). Tonight is a big earnings night: Marvell, Salesforce, HP, and Snowflake all report after close. How those go will set the tone for AI hardware vs software heading into month-end.
2026-05-27.market_context: Clear winner vs loser today: AI hardware (chips, memory, servers) is flying while software and cybersecurity are getting hit. Zscaler showed that companies are spending so much on AI compute that it is squeezing their margins — that is bad news for all software/SaaS names. On the good side: SMTC beat earnings and is up 9%, Rocket Lab cleared a $3.5B government satellite program and is up 7.5%, IREN signed a $1.6B Dell Blackwell AI deal. Barclays raised MU target to $1,175 (from $675). Small caps outperforming the Nasdaq today suggests the rally is getting broader. Bernstein conference has JPMorgan and BofA CEOs speaking today — watch for macro comments.

2026-06-02.headline: GOOGL drops $80B equity raise to fund AI — Berkshire puts in $10B. Semis flying, software getting hit. HPE +26% on AI server blowout. MRVL +21% after Jensen calls it the next trillion-dollar company. JOLTS at 10am. AVGO earnings tomorrow.
2026-06-02.overview: Futures are slightly lower after Monday was another record day. The overnight bombshell: Alphabet just announced an $80 billion equity raise to fund AI infrastructure spending, and Berkshire Hathaway is putting $10 billion into it. That is the biggest single AI capex signal anyone has made — it's bullish for chip and hardware companies but it's also spooking software stocks because investors now wonder if even the biggest companies can keep funding AI forever. Hardware is ripping: HPE blew out earnings (revenue $10.7B vs $9.74B expected, Cloud & AI $7.7B) and is up 26% pre-market. Marvell is up 21% after Jensen Huang called it 'the next trillion-dollar company' at GTC Taipei yesterday. On the other side, software names like NOW, WDAY, SNOW, OKTA, INTU are all down 4-6% as money rotates to semis. DG (Dollar General) beat earnings this morning and is a mover. Big night tonight: PANW, ULTA, GTLB all report after close. AVGO reports tomorrow — that is the big one everyone is watching.
2026-06-02.market_context: The $80B GOOGL raise is the story that shapes today. Yes, it is bullish for AI hardware suppliers because hyperscalers are spending even more than anyone thought. But it also means GOOGL stock is getting diluted, and it raises a scary question: if even Google needs to raise $80 billion in fresh equity to keep up, how long can this AI buildout last? That uncertainty is why software and SaaS names are getting hit — investors are rotating to the infrastructure layer (NVDA, SMCI, AMAT, LRCX, VRT, MRVL) and away from the application layer for now. Microsoft Build starts today — Satya Nadella's keynote is a major watch for AI platform and Copilot announcements. Computex in Taiwan is still running all week with MU, INTC, WDC presenting. Tencent surged 10% overnight on news it's launching an AI agent through WeChat — lifting Chinese tech (BABA, JD, BYD). JOLTS job openings at 10am sets the tone into ADP Wednesday and Nonfarm Payrolls Friday. Oil down 1.2% to $91 on Iran deal progress. Bond yields falling (10yr 4.43%).

2026-06-01.headline: NVDA GTC Taipei: RTX Spark AI chip drops overnight — ARM +14.5%, QCOM -9.5%, INTC -6.6%, AMD -4.1%. Software finally breaks out: NOW +9%, CRM +7%, WDAY +7%. Berkshire buys homebuilder TMHC +23%. IBM +13% on quantum. Jobs week starts now.
2026-06-01.overview: Big Monday to kick off June. Nvidia held its GTC Taipei keynote overnight — Jensen announced the RTX Spark chip, a brand new AI processor for Windows PCs that goes directly at Apple's M-series, Intel, and Qualcomm. ARM is flying +14.5% because Nvidia used their chip technology to build it. Dell and HP are up too as the first PC makers to use the new chip. But QCOM is down 9.5%, INTC down 6.6%, and AMD down 4.1% — all losing market share to NVDA. Software stocks are finally having a big day after weeks of getting left behind: NOW up 9.5%, CRM up 6%, WDAY up 6.6%, ADBE up 6.9%. IBM is jumping 13% after Barclays started coverage saying quantum computing will be the next major shift in how computers work. And Berkshire Hathaway (Greg Abel's first big deal) is buying homebuilder Taylor Morrison (TMHC) for $72.50/share, up 23%. ISM Manufacturing at 10am is the first number of a very busy jobs week that ends with Nonfarm Payrolls Friday.
2026-06-01.market_context: May closed as one of the best months of 2026 — S&P finished its 9th straight up week, Nasdaq +8% for the month (best of the year), Dow above 51K for the first time ever. The Iran ceasefire extension (reportedly 60 days) kept oil down near $88. But there are cracks: PCE inflation came in at 3.8% YoY (hottest since 2023) and Q1 GDP was revised down to +1.2%. That is a stagflation combo — prices rising, growth slowing. New Fed Chair Warsh meets June 16-17 and this week's labor data (JOLTS Tue, ADP Wed, Payrolls Fri) will set the tone. NVDA's GTC Taipei is the story all week — Jensen has multiple keynotes and partner announcements through Thursday. The RTX Spark announcement this morning signals Nvidia is going after the AI PC market on top of data centers. Also: Moderna and Merck presenting 5-year cancer vaccine data at ASCO this morning — 49% reduction in recurrence risk for melanoma patients. Big week for MRNA/MRK long-term story.

setups.why: Outside bar into GTC Taipei. Jensen already dropped the RTX Spark chip overnight. Running all week through Thursday with more partner announcements and keynotes. 67% of a $31.9B 7-day dark pool is ask-side — institutions are loading up on every dip. ARM and MSFT going with them. QCOM/INTC/AMD getting punished.

2026-05-29.headline: DELL +36%, NTAP +18% — AI hardware blowout overnight. ASTS -13% after Blue Origin rocket explodes on ground. Final Friday of May, tape quiet, big data already done.
2026-05-29.overview: Last day of May and the market is taking a breather. Dell exploded +36% overnight — it crushed every number (AI server revenue up 757% in a single year, revenue $43.4B vs $35.5B est, EPS $4.86 vs $2.99). Network Appliance (NTAP) is up 18% too on a data infrastructure beat. Okta beat and raised guidance, up nearly 10%. On the down side: GAP cut its sales outlook and is falling 15%, SentinelOne announced an 8% layoff and dropped 13%, and ASTS dropped 13% after Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during a ground test — that's dragging rocket and space stocks. The broader market is quiet because all the big economic data (PCE, GDP, jobless claims) already came in yesterday. Light volume end-of-month day. Oil down 1.3% to $87.72 as Iran fears ease.
2026-05-29.market_context: AI hardware is still the clear winner — Dell's 757% AI server revenue growth proves the buildout is not slowing down. But the market is picking winners ruthlessly: hardware beats get rewarded big, while software and anything that disappoints gets crushed. The AVGO/Anthropic story is huge — Apollo and Blackstone are putting together $36B in financing for Anthropic to buy Google TPU infrastructure, and Broadcom is backing the deal financially. That is a massive bet that AI demand lasts years. Today has 4 analyst days and conferences: CRM, TEM (just got FDA approval for a new cancer diagnostic), LOW shareholder meeting, plus BMY/PFE/RTX. TEM is up 7% pre-market on the FDA news. Next week: China EV deliveries Monday (NIO, XPEV, LI Auto), then QBTS, MRNA, JCI analyst days, followed by SNOW and HON analyst days Tuesday.

2026-05-28.headline: SNOW +38% blows the doors off software earnings — AI is a powerful tailwind. Oil jumps back up on fresh Iran strikes. Data-heavy 8:30am: GDP, Jobless Claims, Durable Goods. PCE tomorrow.
2026-05-28.overview: Snowflake just had the biggest software earnings beat of the season — up 38% premarket after crushing estimates and raising guidance. That is lifting all cloud and software stocks this morning. But futures are slightly red because oil jumped back up 2.7% to $91 after fresh US-Iran strikes overnight, and tomorrow is the big PCE inflation report (the Fed's favorite gauge). Giant data dump at 8:30am today: Q1 GDP, jobless claims, and durable goods all land at the same time. Costco reports earnings this morning. Dell reports after close — a huge read on AI infrastructure spending. Drone stocks are flying after the Wall Street Journal said Trump is considering taking equity stakes in drone companies.
2026-05-28.market_context: SNOW's earnings matter for the whole market because investors have been worried that software companies cannot monetize AI. Snowflake proved they can. Salesforce beat earnings massively ($3.88 EPS vs $3.13 est) but fell because its Q2 revenue guide was soft — investors now want clear AI revenue growth, not just cost cuts. Marvell raised long-term AI revenue targets (FY27: $11.5B, FY28: $16.5B) but fell because cloud capex comments were cautious. The pattern: AI hardware still leads, software has to prove itself every quarter now. Oil back above $91 complicates the Fed's job. Tomorrow's PCE is critical — if it comes in hot (est 3.9% YoY), expect pressure on rates-sensitive stocks. 2026-06-04.headline: AVGO -15% on first revenue miss since Dec 2024 — AI hardware reality check. Semi contagion: MU -7%, MRVL -7%, SMCI -7%, CRWD -9%. Nasdaq -1.25% vs Dow +0.70% = rotation into healthcare, defense, software. Bitcoin -5% kills crypto. SpaceX IPO at $135/share, $1.75T val.
2026-06-04.overview: AVGO missed revenue for the first time since Dec 2024 — $22.19B vs $22.27B expected, AI rev $10.8B barely clearing its own $10.7B guide. Verdict: AI semi valuations are too elevated for any miss. Contagion: MU -7%, MRVL -7%, SMCI -7%, INTC -4%, AMD -4%. CRWD -9% on its own weak Q2 guide. PVH -22% just for reiterating guidance — tape is unforgiving. Bitcoin -5% to $62,575 takes HOOD/COIN/MSTR lower. Rotation is the real story: UNH +2.7% (BofA upgrade), MDT +2% (BTIG), RTX +2%, NOW +3.5%, CRM +2.3% — money rotating from AI hardware into healthcare, defense, software. SpaceX IPO priced at $135/share (~$1.75T val).
2026-06-04.market_context: Nasdaq -1.25% vs Dow +0.70% = textbook rotation. Logic: if AVGO misses with AI tailwinds intact, semi multiples need to compress. Healthcare, defense, and software all catching a bid together. Iran de-escalation is the secondary driver — Trump's comments + House vote on military authorization pushing oil -3.3% to $92.86, squeezing XOM/CVX/OXY/LNG. Key question: valuation reset (AI rev actually above guide, $22.19B vs $22.27B is a rounding error) or start of hardware unwind? Evidence says reset — but 35x forward earnings leaves no margin for error. Watch first-hour institutional response on AVGO. CIEN BMO today. TSM annual meeting. Jobless claims 8:30am.

setups.flow: -14.94% post-earnings gap down, first miss since Dec 2024
setups.why: Massive earnings gap down after the first revenue miss since December 2024. Failed 2U is the bearish strat signal but a 15% gap on an $800B company is significant — institutions may defend it. AI revenue of $10.8B was actually above its own $10.7B guidance. Watch first-hour action: if buyers step in early this becomes a Failed 2D reversal. If it breaks lower, there is a lot of air below.

CrowdStrike missed Q2 guidance on top of the broader cybersecurity sector being hammered all week (Zscaler set the tone Tuesday). Failed 2U bearish signal confirmed by the gap down. Double-negative: own fundamentals miss plus AVGO contagion dragging enterprise tech. Cybersecurity has been the most damaged cluster this earnings season.

Outside bar range expansion into a BofA upgrade on the exact day money rotates hard into healthcare. UNH has been beaten up all year — the combination of a specific catalyst (upgrade) and a macro tailwind (defensive rotation out of AI hardware) is a clean setup. Healthcare was the most unloved sector for 18 months while AI dominated. Today that changes.

Medtronic getting a BTIG upgrade on the same day the market rotates to healthcare. Two-bar up with an analyst catalyst on a sector rotation day. MDT had a solid beat and guidance raise last week. Medical device names benefit from stable government healthcare spending — the opposite of AI capex uncertainty.

ServiceNow is the clear software rotation beneficiary. When AI hardware disappoints, money flows into software that monetizes AI without the capex risk. NOW has been in a sustained uptrend and today's AVGO miss accelerates the rotation. AI workflow automation is the anti-semi trade.

Raytheon printing a bullish reversal on a defense rotation day. The Iran de-escalation narrative is a nuanced bullish driver — lower Iran risk means less headline volatility but more predictable defense budgets, and with the House voting on Iran military authorization limits today, defense contractors get forward visibility. RTX has been quiet for months.

Ciena reports this morning before the open. Optical networking is directly tied to the AI data center buildout — the same infra theme that caused AVGO to be priced for perfection. With AVGO missing, the question is whether CIEN's numbers disappoint (contagion) or show optical demand remains strong (MRVL's $11.5B AI targets suggest it does). High expected move either way.

Taiwan Semiconductor annual shareholder meeting today. AVGO's miss puts pressure on the whole AI hardware supply chain, but TSM is the foundry — every chip that powers AI goes through them. Inside bar compressing into the meeting. Listen for capex commitment updates and any commentary on hyperscaler demand visibility. Jensen's $100B/year Taiwan spending is a multi-year contract for TSM.

Marvell getting hit from AVGO contagion even though its own AI revenue targets ($11.5B FY27, $16.5B FY28) look intact. Failed 2U is bearish but MRVL just had its best day since 2000 (+32%) two sessions ago — this is a giveback, not a reversal. Watch if institutions defend the post-earnings gap fill level. Could become a buy-the-dip setup if AVGO stabilizes.

MicroStrategy is a leveraged Bitcoin proxy — when Bitcoin drops 5%, MSTR amplifies the move. Failed 2U bearish signal confirmed by the crypto selloff. Bitcoin at $62,575 is a meaningful level — a break below $60K would bring more forced selling. COIN and HOOD moving in sympathy.

bulls.why: BofA upgraded UnitedHealth on the same day healthcare is the rotation destination. Stable government spending, no AI capex risk, beaten-down valuation. Classic defensive rotation target.

ServiceNow leading software rotation. When AI hardware disappoints, software that monetizes AI without capex risk wins. NOW has the AI workflow automation story — enterprise software actually converting AI investment into recurring revenue.

Raytheon getting the defense rotation bid. Iran de-escalation (Trump comments + House vote) clears geopolitical premium uncertainty while actually improving defense budget visibility. Defense was left behind during the AI run.
Medtronic upgraded by BTIG on the same day healthcare is the rotation destination. Beat and raised guidance last week. Medical device demand is stable — the anti-AI hardware trade.

Salesforce part of the software rotation. AI CRM workflow story gaining credibility as investors move from infrastructure to the application layer. Beat earnings this week — market is finally rewarding that print.

bears.why: First revenue miss since December 2024 — $22.19B vs $22.27B expected. AI revenue $10.8B barely cleared its own $10.7B guidance. Investors conclude AI semiconductor expectations are 'extremely elevated.' The entire AI hardware complex repricing this morning.
CrowdStrike missed Q2 guidance — cybersecurity sector under pressure all week. Own miss plus AVGO contagion dragging enterprise tech.

AVGO semi contagion. Micron was running on the AI memory supercycle thesis — AVGO's miss raises questions about the pace of AI infrastructure buildout. Down hard despite no fundamental change at Micron.
Marvell getting AVGO contagion even though its own AI revenue targets ($11.5B FY27) look intact. Was up 32% two sessions ago — the giveback is violent.
Super Micro AI server maker down on AVGO contagion — same AI hardware capex concern hitting the entire server supply chain.
PVH Corp (Calvin Klein, Tommy Hilfiger) fell 22% for simply reiterating full-year guidance. Illustrates how unforgiving the tape is — any company that doesn't deliver upside gets destroyed.
Five Below fell despite a beat-and-raise quarter. Illustrates the current dynamic — positive earnings surprises in consumer discretionary get sold when the overall risk mood sours.

trump_stocks.news: Oil -3.3% to $92.86 on Iran ceasefire — Trump said returning to military action requires US troop deaths (de-escalation signal), House voting today to limit Iran military authorization
Same Iran de-escalation trade — lower oil pressure hitting all energy names in Trump's portfolio
Iran ceasefire progress compressing the geopolitical oil premium Trump's Iran stance had been sustaining
LNG names pressured by oil decline on Iran de-escalation — energy scarcity premium fading
Bitcoin -5% to $62,575 — broader risk-off from AVGO miss bleeding into crypto. Trump has been vocally pro-crypto (BTC strategic reserve EO) but market-wide de-risking overrides the sentiment today
Bitcoin -5% pulling Coinbase down with it. Crypto sector in risk-off mode.
Robinhood crypto revenue tied to Bitcoin activity — down on Bitcoin slump to $62,575

trump_context: Iran de-escalation is the dominant Trump trade today. Trump's comment that returning to military action requires US troop deaths is the clearest de-escalation signal yet — and the House is voting to formally limit Iran military authorization. That removes $3-4 of geopolitical premium from oil, pushing WTI down 3.3% to $92.86. The energy names (XOM, CVX, OXY, LNG) that were benefiting from Trump's Iran hawkishness and $95 oil are giving it back. If the ceasefire holds and oil settles at $88-90, the Trump energy trade becomes range-bound until the next escalation.

Crypto names (MSTR, COIN, HOOD) are getting hit from two angles: Bitcoin -5% to $62,575 on risk-off selling from AVGO's miss, and broad market de-risking pulling liquidity from speculative assets. Trump's pro-crypto stance hasn't changed — the BTC strategic reserve executive order and vocal support remain — but market-wide de-risking is temporarily overriding the sentiment. Watch if Bitcoin holds $60K. A break below that level would bring more forced selling in the leveraged proxy names.

2026-06-10.overview: DOUBLE CATALYST DAY — CPI 8:30 AM + 10-Yr Auction 1 PM. Headline +4.2% YoY expected (highest since Apr 2023), core +2.9% — last inflation read before June 16-17 FOMC. Hot print = rate hike back on the table. Futures red: S&P -1.07%, Nasdaq -1.51%. VIX >22. Brent ~$93 on US-Iran exchanges. NUVL +39% on GSK $10.6B buyout. MU/SNDK led $6B+ dark pool flow. CPI cool → yields fall → TLT pops. Then the 1 PM 10-Year auction gives a second directional wave. Wait for CPI reaction, then play the confirmed direction.

Crypto directionally tied to risk sentiment — CPI print today will determine if Bitcoin holds or breaks lower, pulling MSTR with it.

trump_context: Iran military exchanges are the dominant energy driver today. Continued US-Iran conflict is keeping Brent crude near $93, directly supporting Trump's energy portfolio (XOM, CVX, OXY, LNG). Unlike last week's de-escalation signals, today's backdrop shows active military exchanges — the geopolitical risk premium is back on. If Iran tensions escalate further, crude could push toward $95-96 and the energy names get another leg up.

Crypto names are in a wait-and-see mode ahead of CPI. MSTR, COIN, and HOOD are all sensitive to both Bitcoin price and macro risk sentiment. A hot CPI print (risk-off) could push Bitcoin toward the $60K level again, pulling crypto proxies lower. A cool CPI print could trigger Bitcoin relief rally. Trump's pro-crypto stance is unchanged but market-wide macro forces dominate today.

2026-06-17.headline: FOMC DECISION DAY — WARSH'S FIRST PRESSER: Futures flat (S&P +0.05%, Nasdaq +0.50%). QURE +67% on FDA Huntington's BLA path. JBL beats+raises. INTC 18A-P in risk production. Iran MOU not final. $44B ETF accumulation yesterday. Statement 2 PM ET, Warsh presser 2:30 PM ET.
2026-06-17.overview: Markets coiled into Warsh's first FOMC press conference — the week's centerpiece. Futures subdued: S&P +0.05%, Nasdaq +0.50%, Russell +0.15%, Dow -0.03%. Consensus holds at 3.50-3.75% with no change expected; the presser will be scrutinized for forward guidance, dot plot shifts, and Warsh's communication style. The Iran MOU is not final — Trump warned the US would resume bombing if the deal falls through; WTI +0.8% to $76.53 but Brent hovering near $79. QURE the standout mover: +67%+ pre-market after FDA confirmed 3-year Phase I/II data is acceptable for accelerated BLA filing for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease. JBL beat Q3 EPS ($3.16 vs $3.10) and revenue ($8.75B vs $8.61B) while raising Q4 guidance on strong AI infrastructure demand. INTC 18A-P entered risk production.
2026-06-17.market_context: Yesterday's FOMC Day 1 traded constructively — markets held the Iran-deal gap from Monday with the key tell in the ETF tape: SPY $21.8B ask + IVV $15.8B ask + QQQ $6.7B ask = ~$44B ETF accumulation, capped by a single $6.5B SPY ask AH (largest single DP print on the board). That's institutional pre-positioning at scale the day before Warsh's debut. Retail Sales at 8:30 AM ET is the only morning data that matters — hot read complicates the Fed's hold narrative. Then markets go quiet until 2 PM. History says: 2 PM creates the fake move, 2:30 presser creates the real one. MU led total dark pool flow Tuesday at $5.2B (bid), followed by SNDK $2.5B ask and NVDA $2.4B ask. VIX below 19 — subdued risk expectations into the event.

trump_context: Iran deal status is in flux. Trump stated the Memorandum of Understanding is not final and that the US would go back to bombing Iran if he doesn't like the deal. Iran is set to regain oil export rights as part of the framework — which is the key oil price lever. WTI at $76.53 reflects partial deal pricing. Full confirmation by the Supreme Leader (not yet given) would push WTI potentially to $72-74; a collapse would spike oil back toward $90+. Energy names are rangebound until resolution. The ECB cautioned that the Iran agreement alone may not eliminate the need for further monetary tightening, particularly as wage growth is expected to accelerate.

Warsh's FOMC debut is the Trump political economy story of the day. Kevin Warsh was Trump's pick for Fed Chair — his first press conference at 2:30 PM ET will be scrutinized for forward guidance philosophy, balance sheet strategy, and whether he shifts the dot plot. The consensus expects 3.50-3.75% hold, but markets want to know if Warsh is more hawkish than Powell on inflation or more willing to cut if growth weakens. The Iran deal removing the oil inflation lever gave Warsh political cover to hold without appearing weak — markets priced in a hold with the VIX below 19 heading in.

rules: FOMC framework: 2 PM = fake move, 2:30 PM Warsh presser = real move — wait for the real one

Don't trade every headline (Iran deal noise, Trump tweets) — let price show direction

2026-06-26.headline: PCE 4.1% YoY matched est, Core PCE 3.4% beat — fourth straight month of acceleration. Warsh hike-or-hold posture locked in. BABA triple signal: Bearish Gapper + Bearish Kicker + $43.9M put block. GOOG/GOOGL $1.1B combined ask AH. Memory giving back. Friday cool-off — size down, wait for 10 AM data, Trump speech 1:30 PM.
2026-06-26.overview: PCE came in at 4.1% YoY (highest since April 2023, matched consensus) and Core PCE at 3.4% YoY (slight beat vs 3.3% expected) — the fourth straight month of accelerating inflation. Markets digested it surprisingly well with personal spending resilient at +0.7% and jobless claims soft at 215K. Analysts expect this to be near the peak as the Iran/Hormuz energy spike unwinds from the data. The Fed is locked into Warsh's hike-or-hold posture — no pivot narrative survives a fourth straight acceleration month. BABA is the morning's most compelling single-name story: Bearish Gapper + Bearish Kicker from yesterday + largest options block of the session ($43.9M put at $155 strike) — three different signals pointing the same direction. The Alphabet pair (GOOG $620M + GOOGL $500M = $1.1B+ combined ask AH) is the standout on the institutional side. Memory stocks (MU, SNDK, STX) are giving back some of Thursday's explosive MU-driven gains. Light corporate calendar today — UMich Sentiment at 10 AM, two Fed speakers, and Trump speaking at 1:30 PM are the tape-movers. Size down and prepare for next week.
2026-06-26.market_context: This is a Friday cool-off session after one of the busiest catalyst weeks of the year: KOSPI crash → PMI → MU blowout → bank stress tests → PCE. The tape has been through a full macro cycle in five days. PCE confirms inflation isn't done — but the market's resilience Thursday after the print (Nasdaq finished positive) tells you investors are treating this as the peak, not the start of a new acceleration leg. The key tells: (1) personal spending +0.7% is strong, meaning the consumer isn't breaking down, (2) oil is retreating and the Iran/Hormuz risk premium is fading — both are disinflationary forces that lag in the PCE data. The BABA $43.9M put block is the single loudest bearish signal on the board — that's not retail activity, that's a fund making a macro or company-specific bet. Cross-reference: BABA is a Bearish Gapper, was a Bearish Kicker yesterday, and China tech names are under pressure with ongoing regulatory risk. The NVDA $205P Dec 2027 sweep ($2.7M) is a long-dated hedge worth noting — not a directional day-trade but a signal that someone is buying insurance on NVDA two years out. Two Fed speakers (Williams 10:30, Kashkari 11:30) right after UMich data will set the afternoon tone. Trump at 1:30 PM = choppy consolidation. Position size accordingly — Friday after a big week is not the day to over-extend.

2026-06-25.headline: MU blows the doors off — $41.5B revenue vs $35.8B est, EPS $25.11 vs $20.20, Q4 guide $50B. Memory supercycle confirmed. Semi cluster ripping: SNDK +15%, WDC +12%, STX +10%, QCOM +10.7%. Heaviest macro stack of the month at 8:30: PCE + GDP + Claims + Durable Goods all at once. Fed speakers Williams 3:40 PM + Goolsbee 6:30 PM.
2026-06-25.overview: Micron delivered one of the most decisive earnings beats of the AI era. Revenue $41.46B crushed the $35.84B estimate by over $5.6B — the data center segment alone grew 7x year-over-year to $11.5B. EPS $25.11 beat by 24%. Q4 guide of $50B revenue and $31 EPS absolutely vaporized the Street's $43.2B estimate. Gross margins at 84.6% GAAP. Multi-year Strategic Customer Agreements for HBM chips were the surprise pillar — memory shortages could persist beyond 2027. The entire memory and AI infrastructure cluster is ripping pre-market: SNDK +15%, WDC +12%, STX +10%, LRCX +7%, AMAT +7%, QCOM +10.7% after doubling its FY29 non-handset revenue target to $40B with $15B from AI data centers. TECH +20% on a Merck KGaA acquisition. WEN continues its squeeze story — up ~13% today for a ~32% weekly gain. The macro stack at 8:30 is the heaviest of the month: PCE, Core PCE, Final Q1 GDP, Unemployment Claims, and Durable Goods all drop simultaneously. Let the data clear before adding to anything.
2026-06-25.market_context: Micron's print did what it needed to do — it provided a powerful counterargument to the AI bear case and directly validated the AI memory demand thesis. S&P futures +0.65%, Nasdaq +2.09%, Russell +0.12%. The semi cluster is the leadership group today. QCOM's Investor Day yesterday was additive: doubling the FY29 non-handset revenue target to $40B (from $22B) with $15B targeted from AI data center alone tells you the AI infrastructure TAM is expanding well beyond GPUs into every chip category. The Fed stress tests passed cleanly — JPM, GS, WFC, STT, and BK all announced buyback and dividend increases, giving the financial sector a tailwind heading into quarter-end. The critical variable today is PCE at 8:30. Markets are looking for headline PCE at 4.1% YoY and core at 3.3% MoM — if core comes in above 3.3%, Warsh's hawkish dot-plot re-accelerates and yields spike, which could take some air out of the semi rally. A cool print (3.1% or below) gives the Fed room to pause and is the green light for a full risk-on push into quarter-end. Fed speakers Williams (3:40 PM) and Goolsbee (6:30 PM) add another narrative layer in the afternoon — position before, not after.

2026-06-24.headline: Bounce morning after Tuesday's KOSPI rout. MU earnings AMC is THE print of the week. WEN +20% short squeeze (flagged on <3 prints filter yesterday). GOOGL joins DJIA June 29. QCOM acquires Modular + Investor Day 2:15 PM. NVDA shareholder meeting 12 PM. PCE Thursday is the alpha catalyst.
2026-06-24.overview: Futures are modestly green — S&P +0.5%, Nasdaq +0.9%, Russell +0.25% — as investors attempt to stabilize after Tuesday's AI/tech selloff. The Korea KOSPI bounced +3.26% overnight as investors aggressively bought the dip in Samsung (rally to +10% on a potential $59B buyback) and SK Hynix (ADR listing plans, $29B). The overnight semi reversal sets a more constructive tone for US names into today. WEN is the morning's biggest story — up 20%+ pre-market on a new CFO appointment and a massive short squeeze (S3 Partners: ~23% of float short; Trian/Peltz still circling for take-private). The FlowMS edge: WEN appeared on the <3 prints filter yesterday, and the option chain showed heavy OI stacking at the $8/$8.5 strikes well above where the stock traded — filters flagged it before the news broke. Two catalyst sessions define the week: MU earnings AMC tonight and PCE Thursday morning. Everything else is noise until those clear.
2026-06-24.market_context: Technically this is a bounce, not an all-clear. The S&P 500 remains above key support despite Tuesday's weakness — this is a valuation reset in AI/tech, not the start of a broader bear market. The critical distinction the morning summary makes: traditional macro culprits aren't driving this. The 10-yr yield is at 4.46% (down 3 bps), WTI crude is falling another 2.4% to $71.13 (easing inflation), and Brent is down nearly 2.5%. Iran assured no Strait of Hormuz tolls; Trump's DOJ is investigating oil companies on gas price pace. So rates aren't breaking higher, oil is easing — the selling Tuesday was valuation exhaustion on stretched AI/semi names after an extraordinary run. MU tonight is the clean test: if earnings confirm AI memory demand is real and guidance raises, the dip-buyers win. If MU guides down or warns on oversupply risk from Korean competition, we revisit lows. Everything flows from that print. Thursday's PCE is the macro follow-up — futures pricing 38 bps of tightening in H2 with Warsh's hawkish dot-plot, so a hot PCE re-accelerates the rate fear narrative. A cool PCE gives the Fed room to pivot and is the single biggest bull catalyst of the week.

2026-06-23.headline: Global AI/tech selloff — KOSPI -10%, Samsung/SK Hynix -12%+ overnight. Nasdaq futures -2.5%, QQQ -2.6%, SPY -1.3%. Monday DP tape called it: STX $1.5B bid AH, MU $645M bid AH. MU down hard pre-market ahead of earnings tomorrow AMC. Flash PMI 9:45 AM is the first data post-Warsh. FDX + CCL + Cerebras earnings AMC.
2026-06-23.overview: The Monday dark pool tape telegraphed every bit of this. STX $1.5B bid AH (day's biggest single name print), MU $645M bid AH, MSFT $518M bid AH, AMZN $353M bid AH, JPM $317M bid pre — institutions positioned into the overnight Korea KOSPI crash (-9.99%) driven by Samsung and SK Hynix each falling 12%+, triggering a 20-minute trading halt on the Seoul exchange. Nasdaq futures are -2.5%, S&P futures -1.26%, and Russell 2000 futures -1.30%. The selling is concentrated in technology, semiconductors, mega-cap AI names, and hyperscalers as investors unwind crowded positions on rising rate fears, AI ROI concerns, and now a direct Korean semiconductor shock. PRIM is the lone disaster — down 37% after cutting FY26 EPS guidance to $2.05–$2.60 from $4.80–$5.00 with a COO departure and renewables cost blowout. On the bullish side: IBM +4.6% (JPMorgan upgrade + OpenAI partner program), APGE +46.7% on an AbbVie acquisition, and the quantum trade briefly popped on Trump's quantum EO. Flash PMI at 9:45 is the first macro read post-FOMC — let it clear before adding risk.
2026-06-23.market_context: This is a technical unwind, not a fundamental break — but it is happening after an extended rally, which makes investors hair-trigger on negative headlines. Three things driving the move: (1) Korea KOSPI -10% overnight on foreign chip stock liquidation, direct pressure on MU (earnings tomorrow AMC), NVDA, AMAT, LRCX, and the entire semi stack; (2) AI ROI narrative cracking — GOOGL under pressure after Nobel researcher John Jumper left DeepMind for Anthropic, SpaceX (SPCX) down $600B combined over 3 sessions after its IPO, ORCL quietly cut 21,000 jobs (13% of workforce) citing AI-driven efficiency; (3) Quarter-end mechanics — Nasdaq-100 rebalance, pension fund rebalancing, record options expiration all amplifying vol. The DP tape from Monday told you the institutions were hedging: bid-side megacap flow (MSFT, AMZN, MU) plus STX $1.5B bid AH lines up perfectly with the Bearish Kicker calls on STX, IWM, MU, MCHP flagged yesterday. The APGE $3.4B ask (AbbVie M&A) dominated total name flow. Defensives are outperforming: healthcare, utilities, telecoms, consumer staples, software services. Flash PMI + FDX earnings tonight are the two next data points that can reset the narrative.

2026-06-18.headline: POST-FOMC THURSDAY: AVGO $2.3B Dark Pool Bid Headlines Session — INTC +9% on Trump-Apple Chip Deal. Last Trading Day Before Juneteenth.
2026-06-18.overview: Post-FOMC Thursday with light data and a holiday-shortened week closing out. The session headline is AVGO $2.3B dark pool bid after-hours — one of the largest single-name prints ever recorded. Intel surged ~9% after Trump posted on Truth Social about a deal with Apple to design and build chips in the US, pulling the entire chip ecosystem higher (MRVL +7%, LRCX +5%, AMAT +5%). Memory names also ripped: WDC +5.6%, MU +4%, SNDK +4%. Accenture cratered -13% on a $4.175B M&A spending spree. SPY and IWM both closed in inside-day conditions post-FOMC — compression setting up a direction move early next week. Markets are CLOSED Friday for Juneteenth. Today was the last day to position/hedge for the long weekend.

IWM is the top Strat setup heading into next week: daily 2-2 reversal, only ~$2 from the trigger. Dark pool flow context: AVGO $2.3B bid AH is the headline and one of the biggest single-name prints seen in any recap. MU led total session flow at $5.8B (bid), AVGO $4.1B (bid), NVDA $2.9B (ask). The AVGO + MU bid-heavy tape is consistent with institutional positioning ahead of next semiconductor earnings cycle. Intel rally may have legs — but history says INTC struggles to hold gains, so prefer 10-min ORB entry vs. chasing opens. Juneteenth closure Friday means any rebalancing for the new hawkish dot-plot reality already happened today. Next data print: Monday.

2026-07-01.headline: Q3 Day 1 — ADP whiffs at 98K vs 122K expected. BE +7.7% on Brookfield $5B→$25B energy deal. PLTR up on Trump's personal stock buy disclosed. TSLA Q2 delivery count drops tomorrow 9am. ISM Mfg + Fed's Warsh both at 10am.
2026-07-01.overview: Welcome to Q3. First trading day out of the gate and the jobs data already misses — ADP came in at 98K vs 122K expected, which means the market opens cautious and all eyes go to ISM Manufacturing at 10am. Fed Chair Warsh is also speaking at the ECB forum in Sintra at 10am. Same time, double macro catalyst. Futures are soft: S&P -0.18%, Nasdaq -0.47%. The big premarket mover is BE (Bloom Energy) up 7.7% — Brookfield Asset Management expanded their energy partnership from $5B to $25B overnight after market close. That's the strat filter working exactly as designed: BE was flagged yesterday as Inside(1) + Bullish Gapper, closed up 10% on the day, then the Brookfield news hit after hours. PLTR is up 2.26% after Trump's quarterly personal stock disclosure showed a $100–250K purchase. MSFT is down premarket on layoff news (2.5% workforce cut, ~6,000 people) but that's actually bullish for margins. TSLA Q2 delivery count releases tomorrow July 2 around 9am — the straddle setup is today, before close.
2026-07-01.market_context: Dark pool from yesterday (6/30): NVDA $1.0B ask print — institutional Q3 positioning is in motion. C $694M mixed (bank rebalancing into Q3), MU $675M ask (memory thesis still intact after last week's DP flush), MSFT $307M bid (buy-the-dip on layoff news), AMAT $251M bid (semiconductor equipment demand). Options flows: ORCL $200C 1/15/27 sweep $3.3M — someone building a long-dated Oracle position at $200 strike. TSM $400C Sep 18 block $62.6M is still one of the biggest single call positions ever surfaced — unchanged conviction. The 'Filters at Work' story this week is BE: Inside(1) + Bullish Gapper flagged on 6/30 → up 10% on the session → Brookfield $25B AH → now up 7.7% premarket. That is the filter doing exactly what it is supposed to do. Q3 themes crystallizing early: nuclear/energy (OKLO, BE), AI infrastructure (NVDA, MSFT, AMZN), and EV delivery season (XPEV beat, NIO in-line, TSLA tomorrow).

Q3 starts clean — do not start it with a loss by forcing a trade into macro noise.

2026-07-02.headline: NFP + TSLA Deliveries — Last Catalyst Before a 3-Day Weekend
2026-07-02.close_overview: NFP came in hotter than the 100-125K consensus — yields jumped at 8:30 and the tape sold off into the bell. TSLA was the morning casualty: delivery numbers disappointed, opened at $428, tagged $432 HOD for about 10 minutes, then dumped -8% intraday to $389 LOD. QQQ -1.7% led the indices as semis and growth names faded on the hot jobs print. AAPL +4.8% was the lone bright spot. Pre-holiday volume dried up by 11am as desks cleared into the 3-day weekend. By noon, it was an empty tape — anyone still watching charts was the problem.
2026-07-02.overview: NFP at 8:30 is the anchor. Market wants confirmation the labor market is softening — consensus expects 100-125K jobs after last month's 172K blowout. Anything below 100K sends yields lower and gives growth names a green light. Above 130K and Warsh's hawkish narrative returns hard. PLTR is the cleanest setup into this: upgraded premarket by D.A. Davidson (buy from neutral), closed a perfect Failed 2D yesterday +7.77%, dark pool flow is stacked bullish. TSLA delivery day with RIVN already raising guidance premarket — EV momentum is real. NVDA is a two-sided landmine at Failed 2D clustering — South Korea KOSPI -7.9% overnight is a headwind. Memory names (MU, SNDK) continue lower. Three-day weekend means desks clear by noon. Be early, be fast, take profit.
2026-07-02.market_context: Dark pool from 7/1 close: JPM $906M mixed leads the board — financials at top of DP for second consecutive week on Q3 kickoff. MU $649M ask AH (institutions positioning into weakness), NVDA $500M bid AH (dip buyers still present), MSFT $431M bid AH, META $355M bid AH. Options sweeps: AMD $580C 7/24/26 at $6.2M (someone calling for AMD summer run), BABA $155P 7/17/26 block $41.8M (persistent positioning, third week running). PLTR +4%+ premarket on D.A. Davidson upgrade. Crypto bid — Bitcoin above $61K, Strategy +6%, Coinbase +3% premarket. AeroVironment (AVAV) +4% on $500M Army drone contract.

2026-07-07.headline: Semis Roll Over as Chinese Firms Ditch NVDA for Domestic Chips; CRNX +90% on VRTX $10B Buyout; Rotation Out of Tech Continues
2026-07-07.overview: Futures are mixed heading into Tuesday — S&P -0.1%, Nasdaq -0.7%, DJIA +0.4%. The post-holiday rally in semis is unwinding fast: Samsung posted an 1800% Q2 operating profit jump and still disappointed, signaling investors doubt the pace of growth can be sustained. The gut-punch is Bloomberg reporting Chinese firms are actively abandoning NVDA accelerators for domestic silicon — a direct hit to NVDA's China revenue story. Rotation out of tech into broader market and value names is the dominant theme. Meanwhile CRNX is up 90%+ after VRTX announced a $10B cash acquisition at $85/share, validating FlowMS's DP filters which had 5 prints on the board Thursday. CRM +2.07% on $1B Switzerland agentic AI investment. WMT +1.2% after Trump Truth Social post claimed credit for Walmart price cuts. Iran fired two missiles at commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz — geopolitical risk re-entering. FOMC Member Bowman (hawkish, permanent voter) speaks at 7 AM before Trade Balance at 8:30.
2026-07-07.market_context: Tech under pressure as semi momentum fades. NVDA faces dual headwinds: Chinese customer defection (Bloomberg) and a bearish kicker pattern with $2.7B DP ask on 7/3. AMD also printing a bearish kicker with $2.4B bid — institutional repositioning, not accumulation. MU is bucking the trend: $4.0B DP bid led ALL flow on Thursday, the largest single-name total, with an Inside (1) setup coiling. LRCX $907M ask AH is the week's largest single print — semi-equipment names rarely top the board, so that's meaningful positioning. BE continues its multi-week DP run at $780M ask AH, staying on the AI infrastructure thesis. Macro is quiet until CPI next week; Bowman's hawkish tone could pressure Nasdaq further if she pushes back on rate cut expectations after the soft NFP.

2026-07-08.headline: Iran Ceasefire Declared Over — Oil +5%, Nasdaq -1.4%; Energy & Defense Surge as Geopolitical Risk-Off Hits Tape
2026-07-08.overview: The most significant geopolitical event since the Hormuz attacks is unfolding pre-market: Trump declared the Iran ceasefire MoU "over" at the NATO meeting in Ankara, citing US/Iran trading strikes — US strikes were reportedly 4-5x bigger than previous rounds. Brent crude jumped +5.68% to $78.39 and WTI +5.42% to $74.29. The Strait of Hormuz disruption is now the primary market driver. S&P futures -0.9% (-68 pts), Nasdaq -1.38% (-406 pts), DJIA -1.09% (-579 pts), Russell -1.25%. VIX +5.11% to 18.40. 10-yr yield +3 bps to 4.56%. Bitcoin -2.71% to $62,225. The playbook is clear: long energy/defense, short airlines/discretionary, hedge with gold. Memory stocks under additional pressure (SNDK -5.4%, MU -4.7%). BABA +8.9% pre-market on Alibaba Cloud claiming 40.1% of China full-stack AI market. Trump press conference at 12 PM ET is the intraday wildcard. FOMC Minutes at 2 PM ET.
2026-07-08.market_context: This is a full geopolitical risk-off tape — oil shock driving the rotation playbook in real time. Energy (XLE, XOM, CVX, OXY, COP) is the one sector seeing green. Defense (ITA, LMT, RTX, GD, NOC, PLTR) follows as budget spending accelerates. Airlines and cruise lines getting hammered on fuel cost explosion: CCL -3.5%, Norwegian -3%, UAL -3%, DAL -2%. 2026-07-09.headline: Semi/AI Rebound Leads Nasdaq +0.56% as Market Looks Past Iran; ALNY +18% on ATTR Space Win; IONS -22%, AZN -8% on Wainua Trial Failure
2026-07-09.overview: Market stabilizing with AI/semi rebound leading Nasdaq +0.56% as Iran walk-back gives permission to rotate back in. The real story is the FOMC Minutes context: 9-8 split on whether to hike this year — but those minutes were written BEFORE the 57K NFP miss on July 2. Markets are now reconciling a hawkish Fed document with a softer labor picture. CME FedWatch September hike odds now 50-55%, down from 66% pre-NFP. CPI Tuesday 7/14 is the swing factor. Meanwhile NVDA +3.7% and AVGO +4.8% rallied THROUGH Wednesday's Iran risk-off morning — the AI trade is asserting dominance over geopolitics. Samsung +5.6% and SK Hynix +10.5% Asia overnight, SK Hynix ADR 7x oversubscribed. MU +5.28% premarket with BofA bullish reiteration — BUT a $3.2M MU $950P 7/10 sweep into Friday says someone is fading the bounce. JPM $1.8B single print AH is the week's biggest single-name flag and lines up with JPM earnings + CPI both on 7/14. ALNY +17.77% as AZN's Wainua ATTR-CM drug failed Phase 3 — Alnylam's competing therapy is now the dominant ATTR treatment. IONS -22.46% as the co-developer.
2026-07-09.market_context: Semi/AI rotation is back in control after a one-day Iran detour. The key insight from Flowms: NVDA +3.7% and AVGO +4.8% rallied during Wednesday's worst risk-off hours — institutions used the geopolitical dip to buy AI. The DP picture is nuanced: MU $5.4B ask led all flow Wednesday (distribution), JPM $3.2B ask with $1.8B single print AH (earnings positioning for 7/14), NVDA $2.6B ask (still distributing). All three top names are ask-side on a whipsaw day. GEV $479M bid is the standout bullish single print — power infrastructure climbing the board quietly for weeks. META $302M bid AH despite -2.84% pre-market on the custom chip news — institutions are buying the dip. The MU put sweep ($950P 7/10 $3.2M) is the morning's biggest red flag: fade-the-bounce positioning into Friday on the name that's leading pre-market. NBIS $24.7M put block into September is a massive institutional bearish bet on that name. Q2 earnings season: PEP opened the season this morning (beat EPS, in-line revenue, fell 1%). DAL tomorrow. JPM + CPI both Tuesday 7/14 — the week that matters.

Memory is the headline: SK Hynix (SKHY) starts trading in the U.S. after a $26B IPO that came in 7x oversubscribed — KOSPI ripped ~4% overnight, but U.S. chips are pulling back on supply worry (MU -1.1%, SNDK -2.1%, INTC -2.1%, AMKR -2.3%).
