sections.title: Call Option
notes: RIGHT (not obligation) to BUY shares at agreed upon price, on or before expiration
Allows you to buy at the strike price anytime before expiration — no matter where stock trades
Exercise = take advantage of the right to buy at a lower price

sections.title: Put Option
notes: RIGHT (not obligation) to SELL shares at agreed upon price, on or before expiration
Allows you to SELL at the strike price anytime before expiration — no matter where stock trades
Buying Put = Buying Insurance | Selling Put = Selling Insurance

sections.title: Strike Price & Expiration
notes: Strike Price = the agreed upon price at which stock is bought or sold
Expiration Date = the date the contract expires
Example: Call | Strike $120 | Exp 30 days → can buy at $120 anytime within 30 days

sections.title: Why Trade Options?
notes: Options = Insurance for an investor — you don't have to own the stock
Less Capital → Less Risk

sections.title: Reading the Option Chain
notes: Option Chain = list of all available options & the price of each one
Bid Price = SELL (where market is willing to buy FROM you)
Ask Price = BUY (where the sellers are)
1 Contract = 100 shares — remove the decimal to get dollar cost
Cost = Ask Price × 100 | Example: $2.81 × 100 = $281 premium

sections.title: P&L Math
notes: 2 Contracts: Buy @ $2.81 ($562) → Sell @ $4.00 ($800) = +$238 profit

sections.title: 3 Factors That Move Option Prices
notes: 1. Price of the Underlying Stock
2. Time Till Expiration
3. Volatility of the Underlying Stock
Call price goes UP when stock goes UP | Put price goes UP when stock goes DOWN

sections.title: ITM vs OTM
notes: ITM = allows purchase at a DISCOUNT to market price — further ITM = more expensive
OTM = no discount advantage — further OTM = cheaper, lower chance of becoming ITM
At expiration: ITM = has intrinsic value | OTM = WORTHLESS
Intrinsic Value = the ITM portion of an option's price (difference between stock & strike)

sections.title: Time Decay & Extrinsic Value
notes: More time = more expensive | Less time = cheaper (same as car insurance)
Extrinsic Value = amount option price is GREATER than intrinsic value
NO TIME LEFT = NO TIME VALUE — extrinsic decays to zero
Time Decay is AGAINST you as a buyer — you are fighting it every single day

sections.title: Implied Volatility (IV)
notes: High Volatility = large price swings = more expensive options
Low Volatility = small price swings = cheaper options
IV = expected volatility range — like a FEAR gauge for the market
High IV = Expensive options | Low IV = Cheap options

sections.title: The Greeks — Overview
notes: Measures of how option price reacts to: stock price moves, time, and IV
Helps choose strike selection & expiration
4 Main Greeks: Delta, Gamma, Theta, Vega

sections.title: Delta (Δ)
notes: How much option price changes per $1 move in the stock
Example: Delta 0.65 → stock up $1.00 → option up $0.65
Not fixed — changes as stock moves (driven by Gamma)
Further ITM = higher delta | Further OTM = lower delta
Negative delta = SHORT (bearish) | Positive delta = LONG (bullish)
Bigger delta = more directional exposure — subjective to your risk tolerance

sections.title: Gamma (Γ)
notes: Amount by which DELTA changes per $1 move in the stock
As option goes deeper ITM, delta increases — Gamma drives that change
GAMMA NOT THAT IMPORTANT — don't overfocus on it

sections.title: Theta (Θ) — Time Decay
notes: Amount option price changes per 1 day passing
Works AGAINST buyers, FOR sellers — time value constantly being removed

sections.title: Vega (V)
notes: Amount option price changes per 1% change in IV
High IV = more expensive | Low IV = cheaper

sections.title: ATR & Indicators
notes: ATR = shows the day range on potential calls & puts
Use $ATY ATR indicator on TradingView — turn off fibonacci numbers
Use Bigpoppa code strat & momentum indicator (helps with entries & reversals)
EMAs act as support & resistance when stocks are trending — don't focus heavily

sections.title: The Strat Assistant
notes: Used for Time Continuity — make sure all blue EXCEPT 2 up/down bar shape
Turn off ARROWS — clouds judgement & isn't always right
Play WITH the trend — higher probability of it going in your favor

sections.title: VWAP
notes: Favorite indicator — use auto anchor
DON'T TAKE ENTRY AT VWAP LINE — price gets caught in consolidation
Style: turn off boxes under VWAP, switch to circles, increase line thickness
Above VWAP = bullish territory | Below VWAP = bearish territory
Being over/under VWAP doesn't mean you can't take calls or puts — use it for context only

sections.title: Wicks
notes: Wick on TOP = Buyers in Control | Wick on BOTTOM = Sellers in Control
Wicks show WHERE REJECTIONS ARE
LONG WICKS ON BOTH SIDES = buyers & sellers fighting, no clear direction — STAY AWAY

sections.title: Entries & Trade Management
notes: Take entries on 5min, hold on 15min — small timeframe will FAKE YOU OUT
5min crosses ABOVE entry = call | 5min crosses BELOW entry = put
Take entry on the FOLLOW UP CANDLE — not at VWAP (theta kills you in consolidation)
At 10% profit: take it OR set a trailing stop — can always get back in
Don't let a +20% trade swing to -20% — stop allowing that

sections.title: Contracts & Position Sizing
notes: 2 week contracts = slower but more profit potential (time is on your side)
$SPX early morning: $30-60 contracts out of the money & cheap, $40-100 max
Catch a $20-30 move and let it run — buy cheap, sell high
Sometimes you have the right trade with the wrong contracts

sections.title: Consolidation & Patience
notes: Draw the high & low of the range — wait for the breakout, let it close
If ticker goes to a level, closes, then closes ABOVE it — that's a reversal
Be okay with pullbacks — it's going to happen, don't panic

sections.title: Opening Bell Rules
notes: First 15mins is EXTREMELY VOLATILE — trading time: 8:45am–10:30am
Be patient & wait for a CLEAR move
$SPX is very quick & volatile — keep losses small
Long wicks on both sides near open = no direction, STAY AWAY
AVOID THE CHOPPINESS THAT EATS AT PROFIT — don't chase absolute tops/bottoms

sections.title: Morning Routine (5 Things)
notes: Check CNBC → '5 things to know before stock market opens'
Search Market Insider
Google premarket movers
Check earnings in chat
Check watchlist in chat → Zoom call

sections.title: Key Tools
notes: Buy calls @ support | Buy puts @ resistance
Tradytics = cheatcode — goldensweeps section
Get comfortable trading on laptop

strategy_notes.name: JR Psychology Mastermind — Levels to Greatness

sections.title: The Foundation
notes: PUT THE WORK IN: OUTPUT LEADS TO OUTCOME & INCOME
More money will NOT make me a better trader
Stick to the Script!

sections.title: Level 1 — Introduced to Options
notes: Watched YT courses, understand the basics, ready to quit job

sections.title: Level 2 — Callout Trader
notes: Making money from discord callouts but have no clue how — ready to quit job & looking at luxury cars

sections.title: Level 3 — The Gambler
notes: Risking entire account on one play after a couple wins — confidence high = full porting — trade goes left -95%. Damn!

sections.title: Level 4 — Let's Get Serious
notes: Better understanding of the market, know why you're winning/losing, but still inconsistent & not profitable

sections.title: Level 5 — Money Maker but Account Doesn't Reflect
notes: Consistent green days, feels profitable — but bank account doesn't reflect it
Can't pay yourself, then catastrophic losing streak & wipe out
*Something is wrong (could be something small)

sections.title: Level 6 — New Money, Now You
notes: Come back fresh, make promises to yourself — bad habits kick back in, wipe out & blame game

sections.title: Level 7 — The Breakeven
notes: Consistent wins but too many small losses — account safe but hit a brick wall
Not making money but not losing money — don't go back, it gets tiring & hard

sections.title: Level 8 — I'm Profitable (BREAKTHROUGH)
notes: Making consistent money — small losses happen but can't overtake the wins
Paying yourself & you can breathe — all the hard work & sleepless nights paid off

sections.title: Looking in the Mirror
notes: Who are you? Are you impatient? Are you laid back?
Can you admit your faults? Does your ego hurt when you're wrong?
KNOWING WHAT TYPE OF TRADER YOU ARE IS ESSENTIAL

sections.title: Becoming a Good Loser
notes: Market doesn't owe you anything
How do you respond when things go wrong?
Take your emotions out of the trade

sections.title: Small Accounts
notes: Be honest — not a lot of room for error, discipline must be HIGHER
Less is more — target certain days & setups, stop spreading out
1-2 trades is all you need (3 max — rare)
Have a plan & execute it — ignore the noise
DO NOT SIZE UP — when you size down you trade differently

sections.title: When the Account Grows
notes: WITHDRAW — avoid doing something dumb with a bigger account
Trade with LESS in the account, transfer before Friday to keep profits

sections.title: Daily Habits
notes: Have rules & look at them before trading every day
Get early morning activity, prepare the night before
Become routine — everything is planned, nothing is random
What is your WHY?

sections.title: Trading Psychology Rules
notes: You can't be scared to lose
FOMO — don't operate with this, there's ALWAYS another play
Cutting losses = living to fight another day
Instead of 'I took a loss' say 'I PRESERVED CAPITAL'
Greed = chasing hot stocks with no clue. STOP THAT
Not taking profits at your target is a problem — use trail stops
If energy is off — DON'T TRADE. Peace of mind only
Adapt to market conditions. Stick to what works for you

sections.title: Discipline & Patience
notes: Slow & steady — stop trying to hit the 1 home run & it'll come
Work in self discipline & be fully committed
Manage fear by staying disciplined

sections.title: Journaling (After Every Trade)
notes: Explain how the trade made you feel
Did you follow your rules?
Did you break those rules?
What will you do differently?

sections.title: Position Sizing & The Stop
notes: Risk the stop — this IS your true position size
Example: $500 account × .10 = $50 max risk per trade
10% of portfolio × 35% = position size & stop amount
ex: $5000 Account × 0.10 = $500 × 0.35 = $175 — Honest Way
DON'T FOCUS ON THE $$$ AMOUNT — FOCUS ON THE %
As account grows go down to 1.75% — allows entering multiple trades

sections.title: Protecting Capital
notes: Protect your downside at ALL TIMES
Trail stops — set them, don't move them emotionally
If down 10% of account in a day — WALK AWAY. Live to trade another day.
DON'T EVER RISK YOUR WHOLE PORT AGAIN!!!
The cycle: great week → confidence through the roof → overleverage → blow up. Remain intentional.

sections.title: Stop Lying to Yourself (Sam Parikh)
notes: You cannot become a great trader until you stop lying to yourself/deceiving yourself
Know your: account value, risk profile, short term goals, long term goals
There are no 2 accounts that are the same
It's all about learning how to manage your account
Sizing down & expecting 0 can be beneficial

sections.title: Manage to Zero — Most Important Rule
notes: Don't buy a position based on how much you fantasize you'll make — manage your account assuming every position will go to zero
If you aren't okay with it going to zero then don't buy it ★
Instead of $5000 position w/ $1500 stop loss — just enter the trade with $1500 position
Takes away emotion & if it pulls back you won't be stopped out — trade runs the way you want after

sections.title: Risk Per Trade Rules
notes: Know how much you're willing to risk BEFORE entering — small accounts no more than 10%
As the account grows the goal is 2% or less
Risk more with profits & risk less with your own capital
Treat trades with similar capital — don't want to overleverage
Don't ever counter trade (no overtrade) — Trading is NOT gambling, it isn't about making bets

sections.title: The Roll Up Strategy — House Money
notes: You don't have to get everything in one trade
Secure profit & either leave runners OR find another entry w/ a portion of profits (size down)
Example: Trade #1 +$15k, Trade #2 -$5k → Net = +$10k STILL GREEN (playing with house money)
Another example: 3 winners +$5k each + 1 loser -$1k = +$14k total
Use 60/40 rule: only use 40% of profit — if it goes back it's still up 60%
Don't average down. Don't ever counter trend — NOT SMART

sections.title: The Second Account Rule
notes: Profits make people stupid — get a second account
Move profits there or you will buy oversized positions & blow your account
Don't buy LEAPs in the same account you day/swing trade in
End of week: withdrawal on Thursday to bank — keep small % for Friday / next week

sections.title: HOW JR MANAGES — Successful Trader Protocol
notes: If up 600% & trade pulls back to 450% — he sells (trail stop loss)
At 75% profit: secure money. Sell as it goes UP not down.
You're probably not taking profits because you don't like the money — get out of this habit
When you surpass the weekly goal w/ days remaining — trade less & portion of profit
Don't overleverage your profits (respect portfolio)
It's okay to take small losses because when we win, we win!!!
Plan: don't be trading w/ money you need — expenses taken care of before quitting job

sections.title: Compounding — The Framework
notes: Risk management = the continuing process to identify, analyze, evaluate & treat loss exposures & monitor controls to mitigate adverse effects
4 Understandings: (1) Ongoing process (2) Not just identify — DO something about it (3) Monitor controls & resources (4) Confirms risk exists & I will actively minimize exposure
3 Risk types: Preventable (controllable — how you show up) | Strategy (preparation for superior returns) | External (out of our control — scenario plan)
Biggest part of any project is your PREPARATION
SWOT Analysis — analyze the risks

sections.title: Compounding — Controls Table
notes: Account $500   → Amount/trade: up to $100  | High Risk/Lotto: up to $50
Account $1000  → Amount/trade: up to $200  | High Risk/Lotto: up to $100
Account $5000  → Amount/trade: up to $1000 | High Risk/Lotto: up to $500
Hit 40%+ 3 days/week — EXPONENTIAL GROWTH BY COMPOUNDING!!!
3-month projection (minimum +40%, 3 days/week): $500→$3,765 | $1k→$7,530 | $5k→$37,610

sections.title: Compounding — 2% Daily Growth to 6 Figures
notes: You don't need 10 different strategies — discipline + strong why
Starting w/ $2500+: compounding small wins adds up — snowball effect
Use no more than 10% of account — still green with losses because losses are small
2% port growth is the bare minimum. Can you consistently secure 30%?
3 things every day: (1) Know your target (2) Know how much to risk (3) Fill out spreadsheet

sections.title: Nothing is More Important Than the News
notes: Learn to analyze news & think outside the box
Must be able to instantaneously analyze the implications of the news

sections.title: Goals, Discipline & Mindset
notes: Get discipline & stay discipline — be intentional about making money
Your goals & risk level must be in alignment — be open to change & always be ready to change
If they don't share your goals, they won't get your trades — we only care about making money
Trade what's working — don't consistently take losses doing your own thing
No one day should ever make me out going forward ★
Stop trading like there's no tomorrow

sections.title: The Core Rules
notes: Have to stay disciplined within position sizing or things could get really bad — 2 lessons learned
As you grow as a trader — still keep same position size % wise
Position sizes $$ wise will grow as account grows BUT KEEP SAME PERCENTAGE % ★
Small wins can add up — so can losses, especially big losses
DON'T EVER RISK YOUR WHOLE PORT AGAIN!!!

sections.title: The Honest Formula — 10% of Port × 35%
notes: 10% of portfolio then 35% of that amount = Position Size (& risk stop amount)
ex: $5000 Account × 0.10 = $500 × 0.35 = $175 position size
DON'T FOCUS ON THE $$$ AMOUNT — FOCUS ON THE %
As your account gets larger you may not even want to risk 3.5% — go down to 1.75%
1.75% allows you to enter multiple trades — this is where JR keeps his risk most of the time

sections.title: When to Walk Away
notes: If you see yourself down 10% of your account in a day — WALK AWAY
Live to trade another day — because it can get worse
The cycle: great week → confidence through the roof → freelance/overleverage → blow up
Remain intentional. Stop trying to get it back. It's not about one day.
High alerts don't mean risk more — keep risk the same
If you keep your risk down you'll make a lot more

sections.title: Position Sizing w/ Multiple Trades
notes: 2-3 tickers normally — have a plan on how to attack the market w/ your account
Don't risk your stop on too many plays — burn out soon
No more than 5% in a day (2.5% each trade)
Following this breakdown is being intentional about making money
If trade down & cutting into other profit — trade profits (get out of that)
When playing with profits expect it to fail — that's why we use only a portion

sections.title: Scaling & End of Week
notes: When you hit your goal for the week — scale down on position size &/or know when to be done trading altogether
As your account grows you can't overleverage
Be intentional about making money
Following these rules means I will never overleverage & I will be successful
You can go out of the money & trade the plan — have a plan & trade the plan
Don't try to rush the process

sections.title: The 4 Things You Must Know (OHLC)
notes: Things don't just happen on 1 timeframe
4 things: Open, High, Low, Close — where did price open & close? What's the high & low?
Wicks are tales — what is it telling you? Use Strat Assistant on TradingView
Candlesticks happen in domino effect — things happen on smaller timeframe & then move up

sections.title: 1 Candle — Inside Bar (Consolidation)
notes: 1 candle = inside bar = CONSOLIDATION
If it feels choppy on a smaller timeframe it's consolidating on a higher timeframe
More consolidation could (usually dots) set up for bigger moves
1 candles are also known as actionable signals — a lot can be done off 1 candle
Friday contracts = scalp mode

sections.title: 2 Candle — Directional
notes: 2 Candle: Directional — market moves in a direction, this IS the direction of market
You don't need the whole move to be in profit — you just needed that set up
Put trail stop on at least when in profit especially when play is doing numbers
How to trail stop on Robinhood: Sell → 3 dots right corner → enter the percentage

sections.title: Fail 2U / Fail 2D — Who Has Control?
notes: Fail 2U or Fail 2D are based on WHERE the candle closes
Whether the previous candle needs to close below that (and vice versa) to stay 2D
Example: previous candle $424.12 & current candle closes at $424.61 = failed 2 down
The WICKS make it 2D but the CLOSE makes it 2U (vice versa) — which means failed 2D
Sellers lost control → if in put, sell (showing bullish reversal)
Buyers lost control → if in call, look to sell (showing bearish reversal)

sections.title: 3 Candle — Outside Bar
notes: 3 candle = outside bar — takes out BOTH sides
For a candle to turn into a 3 it must be a failed 2
Anytime you go above the candle it's 2U
Print out the strat combos — Timeframe Analysis is key

sections.title: Timeframe Continuity
notes: Timeframe Continuity is important because it's easier to trade WITH the trend than against it
If you want to swing trade something for 1 week — don't buy weekly expiration
Need 2 weeks minimum but 3-4 weeks is ideal — need time to develop, there will be pullbacks
Buy double the time ★
You don't have to hold it if it's up a certain amount — get out/take profit & leave runners
If we break below 50 on 6 month timeframe it means we are coming down — 50% marks very helpful
Get bigger move going with overall trend

sections.title: Top Down Analysis
notes: Higher Timeframe Analysis gives you an overall bias
When looking at a chart — what is the higher timeframe showing you?
What is the larger picture? Knowing this makes trades more successful
All depends — entry on 30min doesn't mean you don't check other timeframes before entering
Swing trading: trading on weekly → buy 2-3 weeks out | trading on monthly → buy 2 months out
Give yourself time to let the position play out — something may trigger off a higher timeframe, pullback & then run your way

sections.title: The 2 Hour Timeframe
notes: Prefer to use for power hour (1pm-3pm) — helps find entry & avoid consolidation
Time that most money is lost is MID DAY — wait until second half
Best times: 8:30-10am & 1pm-3pm
It's less candles on chart — easier to see what price action is doing
Set Strat alerts (money call) for 2 hour timeframe
Don't try to go for homerun at beginning of day — can leave couple runners for potential grand slams
Don't overstay your welcome. Get your piece & get out.
Any small movements at end of day push contract price up fast
NOT FOR MID DAY SET UP BECAUSE VALUE ISN'T THERE

sections.title: The 6 Hour Timeframe
notes: Power of the 6 hour — can use both regular & extended trading hours, helps premarket
To avoid getting cooked because of choppiness — buy more time
What you're looking for: actionable signals — hammers, shooters, inside bars, failed 2D/2U, outside bars, money calls
Best when market doesn't really gap — then you can catch the trigger
Very important to backtest
Inside 6 hour is just marking the last 30 min candle (15 min)
You can base hit a lot if not comfortable waiting for homeruns using this timeframe
This will give you confidence to hold your position until you're right ★

sections.title: Open Range Breakout (ORB)
notes: ORB = Opening Range Breakout — helps you be patient with the market
Does apply to any ticker (excluding $IWM & a few others)
Mark the high & low of first 15 or 30 min candle — take the break of that candle
Usually want to see a strong candle in one direction then take the break
Doesn't have to be a homerun — it's profitable & helps avoid the fake out
Only want to be in this trade for 15-20 mins MAX
Keeps you patient & away from hopping in 2 mins into open & getting clapped
NO STRATEGY IS 100% BULLETPROOF
On a shooter → expect 2UP 2DOWN Reversal | On a hammer → expect 2DOWN 2UP Reversal

sections.title: 30 Min ORB with $QQQ — THIS IS MY APPROACH ★
notes: QQQ big days come from targeted days & tech earnings — usually 30 min candle
Let first 30min candle close (8:30-9:00am) then take the NEXT candle break (9:00-9:30am candle)
THIS NEEDS TO ALWAYS BE MY APPROACH WITH TRADING ★
Rules: Risk stop, don't overleverage & no more than 2-3 trades/day
Better than 15min ORB — more consistent & rewarding
15min ORB good for earnings because earnings move fast
Sizing down & risking the stop limits losses — small wins that compound without significant loss

sections.title: Gap Fill Strategy (High Opportunity Setups)
notes: Gap = difference in closing of previous day's price from next day open
What causes a gap? Catalyst news, earnings, etc — huge volume thrown at it
Gap fills 90% of the time but doesn't mean it'll be filled right away
Gaps act as a form of support & resistance
Always mark gaps at all times — look left & see if there's any gaps on chart
Usually there's almost always a mid day opportunity because of pullback entry
How to find gappers: Walter-Bloomberg (Twitter), Finviz top gainers/losers, Stocktwits, TradingView
First thing when stock gaps — look at daily chart & identify key support or resistance levels
Provide volume = provide opportunity
Gaps act as support & resistance but doesn't mean it's going to bounce right away
Always want to mark gap & set alert 'hey possible gap fill'
Respect your support & resistance when you have gaps

sections.title: Kicking Patterns
notes: Simple & works really well with earnings plays because of the volatility
Bullish kicking: close red, open green — kicked all shorts out
Bearish kicking: close green, open red — kicked all calls/buyers out
Can be a swing play — let it happen, don't have to hop in right at open
Best moves: uptrend with bullish candle & gap down OR downtrend with bearish candle & gap up
Favorite setup for JR
When earnings comes out — check which ones may or may not have a kicking pattern

sections.title: Trampoline Bounce — JR's Most Explosive #1 Setup ★ MASTER THIS
notes: What's JR favorite setup? Gapper + earnings reaction = most explosive setups
It'll gap into resistance or support & then price will bounce off that support
Price action still has to show its hands — don't blindly hop in
ex: most traders see billions dropping for earnings & take it to the downside — wrong
Automatically have a bullish bias: price gapped into support area (bullish bias) → price ends up gapping up
When it shows it's usually an explosive move
Calls from gapping down/pulling back from resistance are MORE explosive than puts
Want to add/set alerts letting you know it's exiting the order block
Need to be thinking: when it goes crazy to upside (bearish bias) & crazy to downside (bullish bias)
Doesn't just work with earnings — works with a lot of things
When it works you can catch 500% move ★

sections.title: Order Blocks & Gaps on Higher Timeframes
notes: When looking at tickers — mark order blocks, be mindful of them
Best way to chart is the NIGHT BEFORE with your set up already ready
Mark high/low of order block & mark 50% of order block
If no order block on daily → check weekly → if nothing on weekly → check monthly
On higher timeframe (weekly or monthly) mark that on there
When drawing order blocks always look left to see if there's any gaps to be filled
Main levels = 50% marks — be mindful of 50% level or you will miss out
Draw Order Blocks off daily: high, low, 50%
Order block levels = price magnets
Takes trading to another level

sections.title: PreMarket Entries & Order Block Spacing
notes: Always have extended hours on — premarket can act as a key for showing support & resistance
Have to understand there's premarket highs & lows
Most time JR is taking entries off the 30min timeframe
Ask yourself: what is the ATR?
Love to do when range is too big — use last 30 min candle from premarket or hourly candle & gives the entry
FAILED 2's ARE ONE OF HIS FAVORITE SIGNALS ★
Always want to mark premarket highs/lows — give them their own colors
$QQQ one of his favorite tickers to trade
Make sure spacing on candles are good
All Order Blocks are just another form of support & resistance — are you approaching key support or resistance? Do you have more room to run?
$IWM gives you 15 mins sometimes

sections.title: $IWM Cheatcode — Patience Pays
notes: Patience pays — Pre-CPI rinse & repeat. See a money call, take the trigger & hold until 500%+. Get 12/year, trust & back test — it's almost bulletproof
Buy cheapest premium while also taking your setup. Look at failed 2U on 30min & 5min
Premium cheapest at the pullback (into the resistance for a bearish/put). Risk the stop — you won't get shaken out
Buy OTM so contracts aren't really affected by pulling back — risk less money, buy towards target price
★ NOT everyday unless FOMC, PRE-CPI, Earnings — anticipate price to run, buy OTM (not ITM or ATM) = cheaper
If price moving left or right it's just consolidation (choppy) — draw a box. Once it breaks out it usually rips or flushes. Also have order blocks drawn
NO REASON FOR FOMO — can make a living just targeting news

sections.title: Price Action Momentum Technique
notes: Core strategy = momentum loss through candle sizes & shapes (YT: Nystrade — A Price Action Trading Strategy That Will Change The Way You Trade)
Identify a key level first — KEY to spotting high probability reversal trades
Want candles getting SMALLER as they approach support or resistance — price is losing momentum
You want price LOSING MOMENTUM when approaching key levels — very key point
Key levels = order block or pivot point. When option chain freezes don't chase the play
NO CATALYST = NO TRADE — catalyst offers easy price action. Do this consistently & focus on the compound, not the money
Don't take for granted you're making money pushing buttons. Want 100% ATR = strong moves
Understand compounding & risk management — the wins outweigh the losses. Compound excel sheet = blueprint to money

sections.title: $IWM Rectangle Box Setup
notes: Mark high & low of last 30min premarket candle (8:00-8:30am) — draw rectangle box
$IWM likes to break out one way & go another — opening print is a trigger as well
When price retraces 50% of previous candle that's also an alert to get in
Settings: rectangle box + weak magnets on, extend left & right, middle line turned on
Price usually fakes out — dash line (50% mark) is your trigger. Safe trigger = breaks 50% mark
Scenario #1: price breaks high → want it to come back through 50% line. Price opens & drops → want it to come back up (fake out) → once through dash line that's your trigger
$IWM 3 bar special: doesn't happen everyday but happens a lot. If price never fakes out, exit contract & re-enter in right direction. $IWM will sometimes break the 30min candle & give the 3 bar

sections.title: 10 Min ORB Strategy
notes: $IWM = bread & butter + 1-2 catalyst. Be patient & let 10min ORB take place — no buying into resistance or support OB
Don't buy calls into resistance or puts into support because of FOMO
Let first 10min candle close then take break out of next candle. Remain consistent — don't let one day get you down
Key: premarket high & low for sell off. Risk stop loss & see it through

7 Hour Strategy: 9am new candle opens & helps market set you up. Looking for 3-2 more. Look left to see where price bounces off same levels. Key: size of candle + ATR helps with holding position — similar as 4H chart
Traders w/ most consistency focus on IWM — most success & port growth. Have to take profits
Choppy hours 10-11am — be done early & get back in around noon. No more than 20% of profit

sections.title: $IWM So Easy A Caveman Can Do It
notes: Risk-management = King. 10min ORB = Scalp — taking break of first 10min candle close. Maybe a fake out — don't be afraid to cut & jump in correct direction. Winner outweighs loser
Days with Data @ 9am = be patient. Check if Trump is scheduled to speak — it'll say 'open press'
Data = Forexfactory. Know key dates: CPI, FOMC, PCE, PPI, or Opex week
Higher timeframes: do you have a money call? (3 bar, Failed 2, 2-2 rev) → analyze daily candle
Favorite setups: 6HR timeframe — 2-2-rev. 6HR & 30min ORB = same trigger. #3 max OTM for $IWM

sections.title: Favorite Strat Setup — Alignment & Swings
notes: Figure out your favorite strat setup — you want things to ALIGN. ex: trending up + bullish order block = could continue running
Treat events the same way you would earnings (straddle w/ calls & puts)
Only looking to swing weeklies if ticker has an event Wednesday → get in that Friday & hold until event comes
Catalyst watch highlights important events the previous Friday for upcoming week
Go to Strat documents every Sunday & you will have all your favorite setups ready
Scan for failed 2 turned 3 — love doing swings for Failed 2's. Prefer higher timeframes

★★★ Watch to Understand Order Blocks (Smart Money Concepts): Ultimate Smart Money Concept Trading Course · The Only Smart Money Concepts Indication You Need · Smart Money Concepts Tutorial for Beginners

sections.title: How to Grow Account w/ High Impact
notes: Focus on days when news drops during market hours — 10am EST (9am CST). All you're waiting for is 30min candle to break
It's not about homeruns — it's about targeted moves w/ easy price action
Take notice of anytime Powell speaks — Pre-CPI last 30min is key
Go lite on FOMC days (if it pays it pays, if it doesn't it doesn't). Powell speaks @ 1:30pm CST
Be mindful of consolidation 3 trading days before FOMC. Every trade won't win but these days are easier — if choppy, call it a day. Sometimes the best trade is NO TRADE AT ALL
Favorite: when news drops during market hours — can run it up once a month off this play alone
Don't trade until 9am CST — don't trade first 30min, give it a min then get in
DAYS TO AVOID: OPEX FRIDAY / THE 3RD WEEK. Choppiness. After big moves the next day (avoid)

sections.title: Swing Trading When You Can't Day Trade
notes: If you're consistent w/ $IWM it'll be consistent with you — in order to get in the play you have to let it trigger. No trigger = No trade
Sell into strength (it can bounce). Get out before 8:15 (less than 15min in market). Always trade $IWM along w/ catalyst plays
TRADE NEWS — catalyst always gives extra pop. News gives small bias but don't blindly jump in without price action
Rather trade w/ overall trend — get a bigger move instead of trying to scalp. DON'T OVER COMPLICATE IT
If you want to take a trade after making money — size down
Post Earnings Swing Method: 1 month out, goal 6-8 weeks. Give yourself time — could take a month for gap to fill (buy time). Can get OTM contracts. Have a separate account for swing trading
Don't let swings turn red after being up 100%. ex: 20 contracts → sell 13-15, walk away w/ profit & leave runners
Swing trading doesn't mean hold the entire time. Volatility increases around earnings but won't be as clean — can trend 1 day to couple weeks. If comfortable w/ profits, take them
Can't be scared of little pullbacks. Can straddle — if puts losing money (cut them), don't hold until $0. Can get weeklies as well as long term swings

sections.title: Cascilda Gameplan — Entry Pullback Technique
notes: Instead of waiting for the trigger, she's taking the pullback (buying into resistance) @ the pullback when it's cheapest — anticipating
Risk your stop because even when it's pulling back you're not going to get shaken out of the play
When you buy cheaper contracts the risk is a lot lower on pullback — ex: $2 contracts can't pull back as much as $150 contracts
Buying OTM so contracts not affected by the pullback — she's buying out the money
★ Why OTM? Because she's ANTICIPATING price to go back (buying towards your target price)
NOT DOING EVERYDAY — ONLY w/ CATALYST PLAYS because anticipating PRICE TO RUN (price usually runs on catalyst, earnings & news)

sections.title: RUT/IWM End of Day Straddle
notes: Straddle at end of everyday — both sides. IWM in the money or RUT @ $200/contracts. Wait for the 3 bar in the morning
Take 40% on the initial move then 300+ after the 3 bar
Went 14/16 times testing it — add to watchlist
Don't go too far out the money on RUT — best luck w/ $185-250/contracts range
For IWM: just get in the money or $1-2 out to make sure you get all of the 3 bar when it hits
Straddle at end of day

sections.title: Cheatcode Part Two — System as a Team
notes: Think of system as a team (no half assing) — all components must work together for this to work
TARGET TIMES = TARGETED DAYS — get used to trading this consistently. Don't worry about the money, it won't happen overnight
Give into compounding. Get out of the money → choose contracts @ the ATR (price targets)
On target days get OTM cheap contracts — ex: contracts $2-4 each, get 12-30 contracts
Easy price action to take advantage of — don't worry about the money

sections.title: What Heatseeker Is (& Is Not)
notes: Visual map of dealer exposure — shows where price is pulled, pinned, stalled, or explosively moves
Heatseeker = context engine (dealer positioning context). Price action = trigger. You = risk manager
Shows WHERE price is likely to react — NOT WHEN. Shows where pressure exists, not what price must do
NOT: a signal system · a prediction engine · a standalone strategy · a guaranteed profit tool
Markets aren't random — dealers hedge → hedging moves price. Follow dealer pressure, not noise
Market Participants: Dealers (MMs) = liquidity providers, hedge risk, move price | Institutions = large, slow, anchor levels | Customers (retail) = chaotic flow that forces dealer hedging

sections.title: Node Types & Color Logic
notes: Each strike = node w/ value + color (absolute value determines color). Higher value = stronger pull
Green → Yellow → Purple = higher volume. Blue → Purple = higher volume
King Nodes: highest value nodes. Where price often gravitates. Late day → pin. Early touch → reject/drive away
Purple Nodes: volatility amplifier. Fast spikes & dumps. Often sharp reversals
Yellow Nodes: volatility dampener. Price slows/pins. Good for reversals & range trades
Gatekeeper Nodes: act as barriers before King Nodes. Test + fail → map reshuffle. Often mark early reversals
Hedge Nodes: appear during macro/news. Far from price. Slow protective positioning. Closer = more influence
★ Color does NOT define importance — MAGNITUDE DOES ★

sections.title: Magnet Physics & Freshness Rule
notes: Far from node = weak pull. Near node = strong pull. At node = stall/bounce/reject/pin
Think ZONES, not exact lines
Nodes attract & repel — trending day: price flows toward big node (momentum works) | Range day: between yellow nodes (expect expansion) | Breakout day: fast entry into purple (fade edges)
Freshness Rule: 1st touch = strongest/best. 2nd touch = weaker (66%). 3rd+ touch = weak/unreliable. Favor untouched nodes
Rate of Change: Rapid add = strong magnet | Rapid unwind = level weakens | Fast shifts = spikes/reversals
More tests = less bounce power. Watch speed of exposure change

sections.title: Correct Hierarchy & Node Priority
notes: Correct Hierarchy — NEVER break this: 1. Price Action → must agree  2. Heatseeker Context → validates  3. Risk:Reward → decides
If ANY layer fails = no trade
Node Priority (Gravity Model): 1. King Node → largest absolute value  2. Gatekeeper Node → deflection zone  3. Cluster/stacks → pin/chop zones  4. Single isolated nodes → lowest priority
Midpoints = MM trap zone (worst R:R) — middle of range = uncertainty. Best trade = fade edges, not midpoint

sections.title: King Node (Daily Anchor) & Gatekeepers
notes: King Node = largest node on map. Dealers prefer price near here by EOD/EOW. Treat as primary destination
Early-day touch → reject. Late-day approach → pin
Gatekeeper Nodes: sit between price & King Node. Test + reject → bias shift. Early rejection = high-prob reversal
If SPX, SPY, QQQ don't agree at Gatekeeper → stand aside
Bigger absolute value = stronger magnet. Mark only major nodes

sections.title: Range, Trade Locations & Daily Routine
notes: Upper extreme = repeated rejection zone. Lower extreme = repeated bounce zone
Trade plan: fade extremes · avoid midpoints · edge = edges
Best Trade Locations: Range edges · King Node area/approach late day · Gatekeeper rejection
NOT: midpoint · chasing breakouts into empty space · inside chop · chasing moves
Simple Daily Routine: 1. Mark King Node  2. Mark upper/lower range  3. Mark nearest gatekeepers  4. Wait for price to reach edge  5. Let price confirm → enter  6. Target next major node

sections.title: Validate a Trade — 3-Step Test
notes: 1. Near a King or Gatekeeper?
2. Price shows rejection or acceptance?
3. SPX, SPY, QQQ aligned?
If Yes to ALL → Valid. If ANY is No → Pass
Case Study Pattern (TSLA/SPY/QQQ): Hit King or Gatekeeper → downside nodes start growing → floors appear below → short pops or target floors. Targets come from rate of change of nodes, not guessing

sections.title: Market Environment Filters & Reshuffles
notes: Low volume → maps distort. OPEX/rebalancing → nodes weaken. News → price moves first, map updates later
Trade lighter or wait during these conditions
Map Reshuffles: nodes can appear, vanish, or move intraday. Pause, re-evaluate & DON'T assume old levels matter
If map suddenly changes = dealer positioning shifted. Do this: 1. Stop back  2. Mark new King Node  3. Mark new Gatekeeper  4. Wait for structure to stabilize
Common reshuffle triggers: CPI · FOMC · Power Hour · Big volatility events
OPEX (3rd Friday): nodes weaken temporarily · map distorts · position resets · After OPEX → clarity improves. Trade lighter

sections.title: Confluence Rule — Critical for 0DTE
notes: SPX + SPY + QQQ aligned → tradeable. Mixed signals → stand down. No confluence = low probability
Confluence Boosters: GEX + VEX overlap · SPX + SPY + QQQ alignment · Price action confirmation
More confluence = higher probability
Power Hour (2:30-3:00 CST): forced liquidations / dealer flows → volatility spikes. Can break or accelerate nodes. Often mechanical, not organic. Reduce size / widen stops
Core Rules: absolute value > color · fresh nodes > reloaded · avoid midpoints · track King nodes daily · watch catalyst (PPI, OPEX, news)

sections.title: Common Mistakes & Winning Mindset
notes: Common Mistakes: treating heatseeker as a signal · ignoring price structure · trading w/o index alignment · expecting perfect symmetry · emotional/revenge trades
Winning Mindset: Heatseeker = contextual intelligence. Price action = trigger. You = risk manager
★ Calm > Clever. Process > Prediction. Edges > Guesses
Nodes = levels of interest — price confirms entry. Asymmetric R:R only. Stay fluid, not biased. Don't fight reshuffles
How to combine w/ your strategy — Heatseeker is strategy-agnostic. Use it with: price action · liquidity sweeps · key Fib retracements · breakout/breakdown · overflow/delta

How long to get good: Week 1-2 = learn node types & color logic. Weeks 3-4 = understand reactions & reshuffles. Month 2+ = pattern recognition + confidence. Most traders feel 'flow' after 30 days. Consistency = mastery

sections.title: What Is a Premium Chart?
notes: Premium chart = price chart of an options CONTRACT — shows how the COST (premium) of that option moves over time
You are trading the premium price itself, not just the stock. Even if the stock moves correctly, the option's premium may not
Premium moves depending on: Time decay (theta) · Volatility changes (IV) · Momentum changes · Market sentiment around that strike/expiration
Reading the premium chart helps you understand where BUYERS & SELLERS are comfortable pricing the contract itself

sections.title: Why Premium Charts Matter
notes: You're trading premiums, not just the stock — premium charts show where the option itself is strong or weak so you don't get trapped buying high
Helps avoid buying at the 'top' of a move — if you buy when premium is near resistance you're likely overpaying & more likely to see an instant pullback
Reduces emotional trading — when you know where contracts historically gain or lose value, decisions become logical rather than emotional
Resistance exists: premium often rejects at known resistance. Exiting there protects gains & avoids being caught in reversals
Support entries = better Risk to Reward — when you enter near support, your downside is limited & even a small bounce can make you profitable

sections.title: Core Concepts — Structure, Support & Resistance
notes: Structure of chart: X-axis = time. Y-axis = premium price
Resistance Level: identify levels where premium repeatedly tops out (ex: $1.50 → $2.70). These are ideal EXIT zones for taking profit
Support Levels: identify flat or bounced areas where premium repeatedly holds (ex: $0.80 → $1)
Confirmation: premium often hits support when the underlying stock hits technical support — look for confluence between BOTH charts
★ Mark your charts ahead of time w/ horizontal lines for key levels. Always mark at least 2-3 levels & gaps

sections.title: Sabrina's Setup — Marking & Morning Approach
notes: TradingView setup: always has 4 charts up — 30min & 1HR charts + call chart & put charts
For premiums in morning: start by marking daily chart first
More volume in the morning → buys cheaper contracts
How she marks lows: go to the 2hr mark where it hits a couple times → down to 1HR & 30min
Sell into strength
Minima of 100% plays — be modest: it shows $2.53, sometimes $1.83 → $2.20 for the best. Keep in mind wicking
Don't have to chase — there will be pullback entries

sections.title: Trade Execution — Entries, Exits & Timeframes
notes: 5min = more data earlier in the day. 2min = if getting in 10mins into market open. 15min = later in day, less data
Trade failed 2 reversals
★ Set your sell $1 LESS than target to make sure it closes you out — if it's showing $1.35 get out/sell at $1.34
Set limit & walk off
Support Entries = Better R:R — enter near support, downside is limited & small bounce = profitable

sections.title: Daily Homework — How to Use the Scanner
notes: Homework each night: check daily chart — what does it look like? Chart daily & weekly. Look at flow
Don't have to get in before market closes but you can chart it ahead
Look for an entry — trade things you know. Look for catalyst/event
Set an alert: mark for bull & bear scenario. No bias
Watch on 10min ORB @ open for entry (if getting in the next day)
Great moves when ticker hits scanner BACK TO BACK: yellow then orange = a lot of volatility
Pay attention to end of day alerts: swing plays (PAY) — small account friendly OTM that's cheap. Could mean a nice move in the next day or 2. It has to make sense

sections.title: Finding Flow — Flowms & Querying Bot
notes: When looking for flow: use Flowms + Querying bots to check flow
Flowms: look for 80%+ calls or puts. Querying bots: see if flow matches (aligned)
How to use querying bot (Ask only): /bigflow: ticker
Daily/weekly charts: check if there's any nearby gaps. Check where daily order block is
Check for catalyst/event to see why it's on the scanner: Flowms (catalyst page) · Company website (investor relations search) · Scan Twitter using the ticker symbol

sections.title: Market Open Cheat Sheet — Alert Priority & Timing
notes: Goal: trade high-volume, momentum-driven moves w/ confirmation — NOT EMOTION
Alert Priority: Orange = highest priority · Yellow = strong · Green = moderate
Scanner tracks volume + momentum, NOT options flow
Best window: 8:39-8:45am — opening volatility settles, direction forms, volume confirms participation
Before entering: pull up charts immediately · check options chain (tight spreads) · look for explosive price action · skip weak setups & wide spreads
News: check news immediately · trade strong catalysts · avoid weak headlines
Good news sources: FSNN, Walter Bloomberg, Wall St Engine, Oracle, Trade Xchange

sections.title: Entry Strategy & Chart Checklist
notes: Use a 10-15min ORB — wait for a clean break of the ORB high/low
Enter ONLY after price confirms — don't guess or anticipate
Chart Checklist: support & resistance · higher timeframe moving averages · yesterday's high/low · intraday reaction zones
Avoid buying directly into major resistance or shorting into major support

sections.title: Dark Pool Prints — Warning Signs
notes: Be cautious if you see: huge volume spike · little price movement · massive instant wick
These are often dark pool trades, NOT true momentum
Risk Management: never overleverage · start with small size (starters) · risk your stop

sections.title: Trade Checklist & Trading Formula
notes: Be patient — some setups need time before making the real move

Trade Checklist: Orange/yellow alert preferred · Between 8:39-8:45am · Strong news catalyst · Explosive price action · Tight option spreads · ORB breakout confirmed · Key levels nearby identified · NOT a dark pool print · Stop loss set · Proper position size
Trading Formula: Volume + Strong News + ORB Confirmation + Key Levels + Risk Management = HIGH-PROBABILITY TRADE

2026-07-13.headline: Iran Strait Closure + Memory Crash — CPI & Bank Earnings Tomorrow
2026-07-13.overview: Weekend flipped the script. Friday closed green — then the U.S. and Iran traded fresh airstrikes, Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz 'closed' and attacked a commercial ship, KOSPI plunged ~9% (trading halt), SK Hynix -15%, and LNG carrier traffic through Hormuz effectively stopped. We're opening Monday with Nasdaq leading the decline (-1.0% futures) on memory/AI weakness, while energy is the only sector with a bid — WTI +3.4% to $73.84. The critical divergence: TSM reported June revenue +68% YoY (beat high end of Q2 guide, AI packaging sold out) — AI demand is intact, this is a memory positioning unwind, not a fundamental crack. Tomorrow is the most consequential single day of the summer: CPI print + bank earnings (JPM/GS/C/WFC/BAC) + Fed Chair Warsh's first-ever congressional testimony.
2026-07-13.market_context: Futures: NDX -1.0%, SPX -0.4%, DJIA flat. S&P 500 at resistance near 7,600 after last week's record highs. VIX +8%, Bitcoin lower — modest risk-off shift. Iran declared Strait of Hormuz closed; southern route reportedly still open (Bloomberg) but LNG carrier traffic has effectively stopped — natural gas jumping. China imposed temporary helium export restrictions on Middle East tensions. 10-yr yield +1bp to 4.58%, 2-yr +1bp to 4.22%. DXY flat at 100.92. WTI +3.4% to $73.84, Brent +3.3% to $78.59. Gold -1.2% (risk-off but real rates rising), silver -2.1%. European markets flat overall — defensives (staples, utilities, energy, insurance) leading; semis (ASML, Infineon, STMicro) underperforming. Monday itself is light on U.S. data — the tape is positioning for tomorrow.

trump_context: U.S. and Iran traded fresh airstrikes over the weekend — Iran hit U.S. facilities in multiple Gulf countries and declared the Strait of Hormuz 'closed' (Axios). Bloomberg reports the southern route remains open. LNG carrier traffic has effectively stopped, natural gas prices jumping. This is the most significant Hormuz escalation in years.

The 'yo-yo' pattern continues: ceasefire signals from last week are gone. Oil is now pricing in a meaningful disruption premium (WTI +3.4%, Brent +3.3%). This feeds directly into tomorrow's CPI — any oil-driven inflation surprise keeps the Fed hawkish and complicates new Fed Chair Warsh's first testimony to Congress. Iran is now the dominant swing factor for rates, not just energy.

2026-07-14.headline: Triple-Header: CPI + Bank Earnings + Warsh — IBM -20%, GS Blowout, Oil Holds Bid
2026-07-14.overview: Triple-header Tuesday before lunch: June CPI at 8:30, major bank earnings (JPM, BAC, WFC, GS, C), and new Fed Chair Warsh's first congressional testimony at 10. Banks broadly beat but IBM is in freefall (-20%) after a preliminary Q2 miss. Goldman delivers a record quarter while memory names attempt to bounce after Monday's -6% shellacking. Iran/Strait of Hormuz keeps oil elevated (Brent $87+, WTI $80+) — making today's CPI especially market-moving with energy already back in the picture.
2026-07-14.market_context: S&P futures -0.08%, Dow futures -176pts (IBM drag), Nasdaq futures +0.43% as semis stabilize. Oil holds bid on Trump's Strait of Hormuz reinstatement — Brent $87+, WTI $80+. Treasury 10-yr at 4.62%. Asia mixed-constructive: Japan Topix +0.79%, Nikkei +0.74%; China June trade beat (exports +27%, imports +36%). Europe broadly lower on energy/inflation concerns. Warsh's 10am testimony = second volatility event after CPI.

2026-07-15.headline: PPI Day + Bank Earnings Wave 2 — AEHR Bullish Kicker, ASML Blowout, PYPL +19% on $53B Acquisition Offer
2026-07-15.overview: Wednesday opens with PPI at 8:30 as the macro anchor — cooler CPI yesterday knocked July hike odds to ~17% but September is still live near 60%. The data keeps coming. ASML delivered a monster beat-and-raise (€9.3B vs €8.8B estimate, full-year guide raised to €43-45B from €36-40B), which is a constructive read-through for the entire chip complex heading into TSM tomorrow. PayPal is surging +19% in premarket after reports that Stripe and Advent International submitted a $60.50/share takeover proposal valuing the company at $53 billion. Bank earnings continue with MS, BLK, BNY all reporting. AEHR is the standout momentum setup after a bullish kicker earnings gap. Yesterday was an Inside Day on SPY — compression typically precedes expansion, and today's PPI provides the catalyst.
2026-07-15.market_context: Futures modestly higher: S&P +9pts (+0.13%), Nasdaq +119pts (+0.40%), Dow +11pts (+0.02%), Russell little changed. ASML's blowout guidance lifts the semiconductor complex after the 15% pullback from June highs — SNDK, NVDA, AVGO, MU, TXN all in focus for read-through. PYPL is the single biggest premarket mover on the $53B acquisition story. Oil holding above $80 (WTI +0.6% to $79.79) as Iran tensions persist despite CPI cooling inflation narrative. Trump held Situation Room meeting on Iran offensive plans — energy bid remains. 10yr yield +2bps to 4.61%. Fed's Warsh appears before Senate Banking Committee (round 2) — additional chop risk around his testimony.

2026-07-16.headline: Earnings Flood + Semi Sell-the-News — TSM -4% on Record Q2 Beat, UNH +7% Healthcare Blowout, NFLX Reports Tonight
2026-07-16.overview: Thursday opens with the heaviest earnings slate of the week and a continuation of Wednesday's rotation trade. TSM posted record Q2 revenue of $40.2B (+34%) with gross margins holding 67.7% — AI and HPC now two-thirds of revenue — and still shows as a bearish gapper. The message: the bar for semis is too high. On the other side, UNH is the standout of the morning, posting EPS of $6.38 versus $4.90 expected and raising its full-year guide — a $1.50 beat that sent the stock up 7%+ in premarket. GE Aerospace beat by $0.42, raised guidance, and is still down 4%. JBHT beat by $0.17 and is up 7%. Eli Lilly is acquiring AataiBeckley (ATAI) for $6.75/share plus a CVR tied to pipeline milestones — ATAI jumping 34%. UBER is buying Delivery Hero in a $14.8B cash deal. NFLX reports after the close tonight as the final major event of the week. Retail Sales and jobless claims hit at 8:30 before the open.
2026-07-16.market_context: Futures mixed: S&P -0.23%, Nasdaq -0.83%, Russell -0.23%, Dow +0.16% as industrials and healthcare offset tech weakness. 10yr yield +3bps to 4.58%. WTI near $80/bbl — Iran risk premium still embedded. TSM's sell-the-news is dragging semiconductors pre-open; South Korea's KOSPI fell 6.4% overnight, Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -9%. Two straight softer inflation prints (CPI Tuesday, PPI Wednesday) have knocked July rate-hike odds below 20% — September is the live debate. Mega-cap tech continues absorbing the rotation: MSFT saw the largest single-name dark pool print of Wednesday at $823M ask-side AH, and Microsoft disclosed it is training its sales force to position in-house AI as an alternative to OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google — a confidence signal in their own product stack.

bulls.why: EPS $6.38 vs $4.90 est — $1.50 beat; revenue $112.03B vs $110.85B; raised FY26 EPS guide above consensus — healthcare rotating back in
EPS $1.73, beat by $0.17; management noted intermodal demand increase throughout Q2 — logistics recovery signal
Eli Lilly buying for $6.75/share cash plus CVR — $2.8B deal; 26% premium to Wednesday's close
$823M ask-side AH dark pool print; bullish gapper on DP board; AI enterprise narrative dominant as Microsoft trains sales force to push in-house AI over rivals

$0.42 EPS beat, raised FY26 guide to $7.65-7.85 EPS — classic sell-the-news; industrial profit-taking accelerating after strong run
Beat on EPS/revs but Q3 guide $2.50-3.50 vs $3.53 est; added $6B fuel cost headwind flagged — guidance shock on the airline

2026-07-20.headline: Mega-Cap Earnings Week Begins — Chips Bounce, Iran Fades, All Eyes on Wednesday
2026-07-20.overview: Futures point to a broad recovery open (Nasdaq +0.75%, S&P +0.39%) as the market rotates from last week's semi selloff into the busiest earnings week of the season. MU, SNDK and DRAM are gapping up in a chip bounce attempt, but the M SMH put block sitting underneath it says stay disciplined. Iran struck for the ninth straight time over the weekend with another US service member killed — Brent briefly touched $90 but oil is now fading as diplomatic channels appear open. DPZ missed EPS this morning despite slight revenue beat; AMC Entertainment beat on both top and bottom line. Wednesday is the week's real test: GOOGL + TSLA earnings after close and AMD's Advancing AI event.
2026-07-20.market_context: S&P futures +0.39%, Nasdaq +0.75%, Dow +0.21%, Russell +0.69%. 10-yr yield -3bps to 4.52%. VIX -5%. Semis broadly higher premarket: MU +4.38%, STX +4.21%, SNDK +3.94%, NBIS +3.60%, AMD +3.12%, KLAC +2.73%, LRCX +2.61%, MRVL +2.55% — broad chip bounce attempt. Iran ceasefire: mediators proposing 10-day cessation of strikes to revive MoU — markets ticking higher, oil pulling back, diplomatic channels open. DPZ +7.08% despite EPS miss as revenue beat was enough. NOW -1.88% on Underperform initiation at CLSA. WDAY -1.23% also initiated Underperform at CLSA. UAL +2.85% — premiumization strategy narrative holding. NBIS +3.60% on Freedom Capital Buy upgrade ($200 tgt). Friday closed lower: NFLX -8% on soft guidance, chips -4 to -6%, energy the lone bright spot. Japan closed today (holiday). South Korea weak — Samsung/SK Hynix extended.

rules: Don't force Monday trades — biggest setups develop Tue-Fri; if nothing confirms, do nothing

AMD: prefer Friday 7/24 expiry contracts over 0DTE — gives time for the Advancing AI catalyst, less theta decay
NFLX: upgrade does not guarantee higher price — wait for Failed 2D trigger to confirm before entry
2-hour inside bars require ORB + trigger + EMA confirmation — do not buy simply because premarket highs break
Avoid holding through earnings — let IV crush, then buy cheaper options if clean setup appears after the print
A planned trade that loses is part of trading. An impulsive unplanned entry is what destroys accounts.
